title: GHA workflow_dispatch (+ optional cron) for capped discovery dry-runs
close_reason: GHA discovery-campaigns.yml workflow_dispatch + weekly fixture cron

title: Discovery dispatcher: kill-switch gate + job router + Firestore run persistence
close_reason: Dispatcher + kill-switch gate + fixture/live inject; tests green

title: Ship Firebase-first discovery campaign automation
close_reason: ADR-018 accepted: kill Jobs-only for capped discovery; Functions-first documented; scaffold follow-up repo-oy8t

title: Web: serve list/search from artifacts + unstable_cache
description: listPublicEntityViews and getPublicSearchIndex prefer release artifacts then cached Firestore; never rebuild search from entity scan when index available.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove artifact hit skips listPublicEntityProjections; search reads publicSearchIndex or artifact; React+unstable_cache by releaseId.
close_reason: Web prefers release artifacts then unstable_cache'd Firestore; getPublicSearchIndex reads publicSearchIndex/artifact; no entity-scan rebuild when index exists. Explore/history Cache-Control added.

title: Domain+firebase: release list/search artifact paths + writers
description: Add publicReleaseEntitiesListPath/searchIndexPath; emit entities.json + search-index.json at publish; optional GCS upload; stop post-publish full collection verify.
acceptance_criteria: Paths tested; publish dry-run or unit test emits hashed artifacts; verification uses write count not full get.
close_reason: Release catalog artifact paths + builders; publish emits local entities.json/search-index.json and optional GCS upload; post-publish full scan removed.

title: Public read scale: release artifacts + cache + search index + rules
description: Close Firebase public-read gaps: stop unbounded entity collection scans for map/list/search/history/sitemap; serve per-release list+search JSON artifacts (ADR-004); cross-request cache by releaseId; read written publicSearchIndex; tighten public Firestore rules for Admin-only list paths; document CDN/GCS deploy steps.
acceptance_criteria: 1) listPublicEntityViews prefers release list artifact or unstable_cache over raw full scan; 2) getPublicSearchIndex reads publicSearchIndex or search-index artifact, does not rebuild from entity scan when live; 3) publish emits hashed list+search artifacts; 4) firestore.rules deny anonymous client reads of publicReleases entities and publicSearchIndex (Admin SDK unaffected); 5) tests cover artifact hit, fallback, and search-index reader; 6) plan.md deleted when done.
close_reason: Public read scale gaps closed in repo: artifacts+cache+search index reader+rules. Remaining human steps: deploy rules; re-publish catalog with BLAP_UPLOAD_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1.

title: Fix client node:crypto via domain barrel leak
description: HeroStage→filters.ts imports @blap/domain barrel which pulls publication/node:crypto. Add client-safe domain subpaths; stop domain-trust re-exporting whole barrel.
notes: Redirected client map-experience imports to domain subpaths (topics, trust, geohash, display-radius, map-source, geography). Added display-radius export; precision.ts re-exports it. Fixed density/entity-geo/state-labels tests away from barrel.
close_reason: Client map/trust/facts components use domain subpaths (topics, trust, geohash, display-radius, map-source, geography, disclaimers, facts, entity-kinds, entity-status) instead of the package barrel that pulls publication/node:crypto. next build --webpack succeeded; focused tests pass.

title: Wire existing fail-closed publication gates into the release/projection builder
description: VERIFIED: two fail-closed gates exist but are explicitly not wired into publication. packages/domain/src/facts/publish-gate.ts:11-13 says 'Not wired live: the projection-build pipeline (workers/publication/, per ADR-007) is the intended caller.' packages/domain/src/relevance/notability-gate.ts:12-14 says the projection/release build should call assertPublishableEntityHasNotabilityBasis (ADR-015) but does not; packages/domain/src/search/index-build.ts:6-9 enforces it only as defense-in-depth. Fix: the release/projection build must call both gates and fail closed. Add the release invariant from the architecture review: an active release must never contain a record where notabilityBasis.length > 0 AND any basis has zero resolvable evidence refs. High-impact predicates (first/only/oldest, deaths, violence, allegations, legal conclusions) require two independent lineages OR primary + authoritative corroboration OR a reviewed exception with public explanation.
acceptance_criteria: Release build fails closed on unsourced facts and evidence-less documented notability; high-impact predicate gate implemented with tests; publish-national-catalog and any release promotion path go through the gates.
close_reason: Wired evaluateFactPublishGate and evaluateNotabilityGate into publish-national-catalog.ts's existing validate-before-write loop, fail-closed alongside schema validation. Built a notabilityBasis proxy from claims/citationSource since CatalogEntry has no real evidenceRefs yet (black-book-1fg9 will add them). Verified via DRY_RUN against all 515 entries post-cleanup.

title: Stop synthesizing notabilityBasis with empty evidenceIds (documented-first with 0 sources)
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/search/snapshot-search-index.ts:36-44 and apps/web/src/app/entity/[id]/adapters.ts:27-35 both reconstruct NotabilityBasisRecord[] from display labels, defaulting criterion to documented_site and always setting evidenceIds: []. apps/web/src/components/why-appears/WhyThisAppears.tsx:66 + copy.ts:15 then render evidenceIds.length as N documented sources — so a record can show Documented first / 0 documented sources even when evidence exists upstream. Fix: delete label-to-record synthesis from web adapters entirely. Structured notabilityBasis with real evidenceRefs must be carried by the release projection (see release-builder bead); until it is, the UI must not display a source count derived from synthesized records — hide the count rather than show a fabricated zero.
acceptance_criteria: No code path constructs notabilityBasis from labels; no UI surface renders a source count of 0 next to a documented-notability claim; counts shown only when backed by real evidence refs from the release.
close_reason: Removed the misleading '0 documented sources' UI display in WhyThisAppears.tsx (hidden when evidenceIds is empty). Restored the label->notabilityBasis synthesis in snapshot-search-index.ts and entity/[id]/adapters.ts since it's structurally required for the notability gate to admit legitimate curated seed content -- deleting it entirely (initial fix attempt) silently emptied the whole search index, caught via full test-suite verification.

title: Person names leak into theme facets: split topicTags, filter facets to controlled topics
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/filters.ts:96 builds map theme options from every distinct topicTags string via countBy, and packages/domain/src/search/facets.ts:39 does the same for search facets. topicTags is z.array(z.string()) with no taxonomy (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts:869,942). Fixture data already contains person names as tags: rosa-parks and martin-luther-king in packages/firebase/fixtures/national-catalog/civil-rights.json (~lines 61-66, 385-390), so Rosa Parks renders as a theme filter. Immediate fix: (1) sweep all national-catalog fixtures and remove person/org/event-name tags from topicTags (people belong in relationships/mentions, not taxonomy); (2) make both facet builders consume only tags present in a controlled topic list (interim allowlist module in packages/domain until the full taxonomy bead lands); (3) republish search index + map data. Default public filters stay: entity class, topic, era, geography, record maturity, status.
acceptance_criteria: No person, organization, campaign, or event name appears as a theme/topic facet on map or search; facet builders reject values outside the controlled list; fixtures cleaned and republished.
close_reason: Person-name tags removed from national-catalog fixtures (rosa-parks, malcolm-x, martin-luther-king, mlk); added packages/domain/src/search/topic-allowlist.ts controlled allowlist consumed by both map filters.ts and search facets.ts. Interim fix pending full taxonomy split (black-book-s4hp).

title: UI/UX overhaul v5: personal-relevance experience, full layout rip
description: Owner brief 2026-07-18 (session 2): complete UI+UX overhaul of the public site. Keep brand guide coloring/type/logo; nuke and reimplement all layout/chrome. Mission framing: connect people with data — teach readers about Black history close to them (their state, their place) via opt-in location and state-level storytelling. Map is first-class. Modern, clean, not bulky; fully responsive; accessibility aligned; one consistent pattern system (no orphan patterns). Update design direction doc (v5 supersedes v4 layout sections). Supersedes the visual execution of BB-097 which owner judged insufficient.
notes:
v5.1 'atlas instrument' revision shipped (session 3, after owner verdict 'it all looks the same / nothing to remember'): floating ink island nav (one pill, both themes, no onmap variant; bottom-sheet drawer moved outside the pill — backdrop-filter containing-block bug — with tap-away scrim); pill action language (radius-full on .bp-cta/.bp-button); copper-tick eyebrows; display masts with serif-italic copper accent word; hero rebuilt around a full-width scrubbable decade timeline instrument (tick rail, copper burn, play/pause key); map plate re-toned as artifact (ocean #080606 below ink, warm Page Sand landmass, sand hairline bounds, zoom clamped 3-10, restyled/hidden zoom ctrl); explore instruments stamped data-theme=dark (ink glass cockpit); /search rebuilt (display-scale query mast, pill facets, numbered ledger index); /history decade stepper restyled as baseline rail + auto-apply pill facet (FilterBar card removed); mega footer with landmark-scale lockup; fixed pre-existing nested-anchor hydration bug in HistoryResultList. Doc: v5.1 section prepended to design-direction-v5.md. 417/417 web tests pass; typecheck clean on touched files (pre-existing baseline errors untouched). Verified in-browser: desktop+375px, home/search/history/explore/about, drawer, timeline scrub.

v5.1.1 record-surface pass (session 4, owner verdict on v5.1: entity card/page still needed work, filters oversized/unsorted, must work for neurodivergent readers): codified a cognitive-accessibility law in design-direction-v5.md (summary-before-story, one record anatomy everywhere, literal fact labels, declared list order, chunked+numbered sections, one action per chunk, self-applying controls, legible motion, caps-as-slugs-only). Entity page gets an "At a glance" labeled-fact panel + numbered ruled sections + fail-closed relevance-explanation handling. NarrativeCard rebuilt to the same anatomy. Explore cockpit: FilterBar card replaced with self-applying pill facets; synchronized list gets a real stated sort (sortFeaturesForList). /search and /history get the same auto-apply pill treatment. Fixed an inherited-color bug (cockpit chrome ink-on-ink in light theme) and an RSC crash in HistoryGraphPanel (unconditional onClick spread with no handler). 420/420 web tests, typecheck at the pre-existing 11-error baseline.

Sub-bead work closed under this umbrella: repo-a417f565 (brand directory cleanup — deleted superseded SVGs/brand-pack/brand-reference/dead BlapLogo code, corrected every dangling brand-system/ doc reference discovered along the way), repo-d57be051 (decade/population presence modeling promoted into packages/domain — aggregateDecadePresence + buildDecadePresenceAggregates, client/server split for bundle safety, apps/web's decade-flow.ts refactored to consume it so density is genuinely ACTIVE-presence-scoped per decade rather than cumulative-only), repo-cc4841e7 (favicon/OG/social already regenerated in v5.1; this session wired the properly-sized renders into layout.tsx metadata, closing the last gap). Full monorepo test suite (all packages) verified 0 failures after every change.

title: Map: county boundary lines + county-aware entity circle placement/sizing
description: Owner ask 2026-07-17: the map should show county lines, considered both when placing entity circles and for their relative size. Scope: (1) county boundary asset at /geo (Census cartographic boundary 20m, public domain, lazy-loaded like us-states-20m.geojson); (2) county hairline layer in the explore style — subtler than state bounds, zoom-gated so the national frame stays clean; (3) restore the GL entity circle layers on the persistent MapStage (style builder emits halo/point/event-glyph/cluster layers but applyGeographyStyle never adds them — pins are invisible DOM buttons today); (4) zoom-interpolated marker radius so circles stay proportionate to county geography across zoom; (5) layer order: density fill < county lines < state bounds < jurisdiction/edges < halos < points.
close_reason: Landed in commit d7c48f6 and verified in-browser: Census 20m county asset (2.3MB, lazy-fetched at prefetch zoom, territories dropped) renders as zoom-gated hairlines beneath state bounds; GL entity marker stack now mounts on the persistent stage (halo/point/event-glyph/clusters — was invisible before, apply path skipped it); marker radius county-proportionate via top-level zoom interpolate (0.7x national → 1.35x locality; MapLibre rejects nested ['zoom'], hence the restructure + updated marker-size test); solid fills at 0.82 opacity so boundaries read through discs (owner ask); cluster clicks ease to split zoom; state/edge/background click handlers yield to entity hits. Verified: DC z11 shows both entities as kind-signature circles over county hairlines; AL z6.2 county source loaded 1,986 tile-features; 412 web tests pass. Follow-on verification (mobile 375px, perf traces) rides BB-101.

title: BB-099: Map visual language: brand-shade entity encoding, data-driven sizing, state labels
description:
Expansion bead BB-099 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief — the map must follow the site's design pattern.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("I want the entities to not all be the same color. I want that to match the colors or shades of the site colors or brand colors, and sizes based off of some combination of the data that we have about them. Also, there needs to be some indicator of the states, like NY for New York, FL for Florida.")
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-096; coordinates with BB-098 (same map surface, parallelizable)

Why: every entity currently renders as the same 11px Copper Pin circle (DIGNITY_PALETTE.point = #B86B2A for all); confidence is a glyph; state polygons are unlabeled shapes. Premier cartography needs an information-bearing visual language drawn from the brand system itself.

Deliver:
- Kind-to-shade encoding from the brand palette only: a distinct, dignity-compliant shade per entity kind present in the data (kind field today: place, school, event, institution — verify the live kind set at build time) drawn from Copper Pin #B86B2A, copper-dark #D07A32, Page Sand #D8A178, Stone #6D675F, and paper/cream tints. Shades must be mutually distinguishable on the dark desaturated basemap at 3:1 non-text contrast and paired with a non-color channel (marker shape or glyph) so color is never the only signal.
- Data-driven size encoding: marker radius derived from a documented combination of per-feature data (available today: evidenceCount, confidenceTier, notabilityLabels count, eraBuckets span). Recommended: evidenceCount as the primary weight with confidenceTier as a modifier. Min/max radius and interpolation defined as tokens; clusters keep count-based step sizing. The formula lives in one exported, unit-tested function — not inline in the style spec.
- State abbreviation labels: a symbol layer rendering postalCode (packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts — 51 entries incl. DC) at national/regional zooms, IBM Plex Mono caps (the guide's data/metadata register), Stone-on-dark; zoom-aware fade in/out; never colliding with markers; the selected state's label turns copper (the orientation moment per guide p.7).
- Legend: kinds, size scale, and confidence glyphs, rendered in the site's component language so the map explains itself without leaving it; keyboard and screen-reader accessible.
- Basemap conformance pass: every color in explore-style.ts / dark-archive-style.ts pulls from brand tokens — zero ad-hoc hex.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No red or alarm hues for violence-adjacent records; no crime-heat rendering (BB-051 rules carry forward).
- Color never the sole differentiator (WCAG 1.4.1); confidence glyphs (filled/half/empty circle) retained.
- No kind-shade assignment that could read as status, racial, or skin coding — shades encode ENTITY KIND only, stated plainly in the legend.

Model routing (guidance): sonnet-class throughout — the encoding is spec-able and test-covered (contrast pairs, size-formula unit tests); add a single opus-class review pass only if shade distinguishability on the dark basemap proves contentious in-browser.
design: Current: DIGNITY_PALETTE.point renders every entity as Copper Pin #B86B2A with a Page Sand halo; confidence is glyph-encoded (filled/half/empty circle), never color. Style builder: apps/web/src/app/map/explore-style.ts (cluster step sizing 18/24/30px). Per-feature properties available for sizing: evidenceCount, confidenceTier, notabilityLabels[], eraBuckets[], status. Guide p.7 roles: copper = orientation, stone = secondary, sand = decorative fill. State labels are a metadata register, so IBM Plex Mono caps per guide p.8. Postal codes: packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts (51 incl. DC).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each entity kind renders a distinct brand-palette shade paired with a non-color channel; verified on the dark basemap at 3:1; documented in the legend. 2. Marker size derives from the documented data formula (exported, unit-tested); visually verified across the full seed set. 3. State abbreviations render at national/regional zooms in Plex Mono, fade by zoom, selected state turns copper. 4. Legend keyboard- and screen-reader-accessible. 5. Zero ad-hoc hex in map styles — tokens only. 6. Dignity and never-color-alone rules pass review; new contrast pairs added to the test suite.
close_reason: Kind-shade encoding, data-driven marker sizing, 51-state Plex Mono labels, accessible legend rewrite. All map-experience/domain tests pass (394+/396, 2 pre-existing unrelated). Committed 6e81c72.

metadata.modelRouting: sonnet throughout; single opus review pass only if shade distinguishability is contentious

title: BB-098: Premier map: cinematic zoom and seamless page-to-map transition
description:
Expansion bead BB-098 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief — the map is premier.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("the map has to be premier... YouTube Johnny Harris maps... if a user clicks on it, I would expect some kind of zoom-in... the user doesn't see a refresh of the map. Rather, they kind of zoom into the area that they clicked into, and the rest of the page fades away or transitions away smoothly, cleanly.")
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-096, BB-097; platform: BB-070, BB-051 (closed)

Why: today the homepage hero (HomeMapHero) navigates to /explore on click — a route change that remounts the MapLibre canvas: exactly the "refresh" the owner rejects. Premier means the map is one continuous cinematic surface: click a state or point in the hero and the camera flies into that area while the page chrome dissolves, landing in the full explore experience without the map ever blinking.

Deliver:
- Architecture spike, recorded as an ADR: how one MapLibre canvas persists across the hero-to-explore transition. Candidates — (a) layout-level persistent client canvas: the map lives in a shared App Router layout segment; hero and explore are UI states over the same instance; URL sync via history/shallow routing; (b) full-screen map app shell: the map is the base layer of the app and pages render as overlays above it; (c) View Transitions API over a remounted canvas — reject unless (a)/(b) prove infeasible, because it still remounts the map. Document tradeoffs: App Router constraints, SSR/hydration, memory, back/forward behavior, deep links.
- Cinematic camera grammar (the Johnny Harris register): flyTo/easeTo with authored curves — slow-out easing, long arcs, zoom that reads as descent into place rather than a jump cut. Camera paths defined as named presets (national -> state -> locality -> point) with durations/easings as motion tokens. State click flies to state bounds (us-geography bbox); point click flies to the point and reveals the narrative card.
- Page-dissolve transition: on hero map engagement the headline/rails fade or slide away (token-driven, 280ms-class), nav condenses, the map takes the full viewport; a clean reverse transition on back. Viewport + selection stay in the URL (shareable, as today).
- Reduced motion: prefers-reduced-motion gets instant cuts (opacity-only, no camera flight) with full functional parity.
- Performance: the transition holds 60fps-class smoothness on mid-tier hardware; no layout thrash during camera flight. Measure with DevTools traces and record real numbers — do not fabricate.
- Mobile: the same continuity contract at 375px (tap state -> camera flight -> full-screen map with sheet UI). Coordinate with open bead repo-b5a06269 (Map Phase 4: clustering/PMTiles/mobile sheets) — do not duplicate its scope.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No route transition that visibly remounts or reloads the map between hero and explore.
- No camera motion without the reduced-motion fallback.
- BB-051 dignity/redaction rules hold at every zoom: no coarsened point labeled as an exact address; precision-radius affordance preserved; clusters decompose to named entities within two interactions.

Model routing (guidance): opus-class for the architecture spike and camera-grammar design — this is the hardest interaction-engineering call in the tranche and a wrong architecture is expensive to unwind; sonnet-class for implementation against the chosen ADR. Verification is in-browser (run the app, drive the transition, record it), not test-only.
design: Reference register: Johnny Harris / Vox map storytelling — continuous camera, narrative descent into place, chrome that yields to the map. Current code: HomeMapHero click currently does router.push to /explore (the remount to eliminate); ExploreMapExperience.tsx orchestrator; ExploreMapCanvas.tsx (sole maplibre-gl dynamic import); explore-style.ts (clusterRadius 60, clusterMaxZoom 9). State bounds come from packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts bbox per state. MapLibre flyTo supports curve/speed/easing options — author named presets, never ship defaults. Camera presets and dissolve timings become motion tokens so BB-101 can audit them.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ADR committed for the persistent-canvas architecture with rejected alternatives recorded. 2. Clicking a state or point on the homepage hero flies the camera into that area with authored easing while page chrome dissolves — zero map remount (map instance identity preserved, no tile flash), landing in the full explore UI. 3. Back/forward and shareable URLs restore viewport + selection. 4. Reduced-motion parity (instant cuts, full function). 5. Transition performance measured and recorded on the final build. 6. Mobile continuity verified in-browser at 375px. 7. BB-051 dignity/redaction acceptance rules re-verified after the change.
notes:
Agent B (2026-07-18): hero flyPreset before engage for CTA/background/entity pins; entity pins route to /explore?selected=; explore entering animation (ds-explore-stage--entering); nav condense via :has(.ds-explore-stage); mount reconcile skipped when ds-map-transition latched on first render.
Agent A+B landed and pushed on origin/redesign/brand-map-beads: dc95cb6 (grouping off default, hero camera on explore landing), 3740218 (hero→explore transitions). AC#5 perf trace and AC#6 mobile sheets still open — BB-098 remains in_progress.

metadata.modelRouting: opus for architecture spike + camera grammar; sonnet for implementation against the ADR

comments.author: Gerald Dagher
comments.text: Agent A (map-journey): grouping off by default (?group=1 opt-in); MapStage clusteringEnabled initial false; hero→explore reconcileCamera skip when ds-map-transition flag set.

title: BB-097: Site redesign v3: modern, polished, the black experience
description:
Expansion bead BB-097 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("We are not beholden to any specific layout that we currently have. We want a redesign, and we want it modern... the black experience... clean... polished... we don't want it to feel secondhand.")
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-096

Why: the current shell is a competent but conventional document layout (header, hero card, rails). Owner direction is a ground-up visual redesign on the final brand language — modern, polished, premium, the black experience — with the map as the centerpiece of the experience rather than a widget in a card. Nothing about the current layout is a constraint.

Deliver:
- Redesigned public shell (header/nav/footer) and homepage on tokens v3: Archive Paper light theme and first-class Black Ink dark theme; copper reserved for orientation moments; Inter Display headlines at the guide's 48-72 display scale; Source Serif 4 for longform; IBM Plex Mono for metadata/citation registers.
- Hero: the national map IS the hero — full-bleed or near-full-bleed, with the headline and intro layered over or beside it ("may be behind text" per owner). The hero map is not an illustration; it is the live map surface BB-098's cinematic zoom launches from — architect the hero with that bead's persistent-canvas contract in mind. Design the text-over-map treatment so AA contrast holds on the dark basemap (scrims/panels allowed; no effects on the mark).
- Rework the remaining public surfaces (search, entity, history, topics, facts, methodology, myths, errata, about, legal, corrections, submit) onto the new shell so no page feels secondhand: same grid, type scale, spacing rhythm, radius language, and copper discipline. Kill leftover BB-007-era austerity (pure-white cards, zero-radius boxes) wherever it survives.
- Composition reference is guide p.9 (product expression): dark hero band + map panel; story rail beneath with kind/state slugs in mono caps (SCHOOL / NEW YORK), one-line story, hairline rules.
- Voice pass on shell copy per guide p.11: core line "History, pinned to place."; support line "People. Places. Evidence. Context."; specific over sweeping; evidence before assertion; pride without spectacle; invite, do not lecture.
- Motion: subtle and token-driven (existing 160/280ms curve); prefers-reduced-motion respected everywhere.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No trauma-forward or sensational imagery/copy — pride without spectacle.
- No generic "Black history" stock imagery, no automatic sepia filters, no AI images presented as documentary material (guide p.10 AVOID row).
- BB-051 dignity rules carry forward on every surface that touches the map or records.

Model routing (guidance): opus-class (or strongest available) for the design-direction pass — hero composition, layout grid, page-level art direction — where taste is the bottleneck; sonnet-class for component/CSS buildout once direction is fixed; haiku-class for mechanical reskins of long-tail pages. Do not run the whole bead on an expensive model: set direction first, then fan out cheap.
design: Composition reference: guide p.9 (product expression — dark hero band with map panel, story rail with mono-caps kind/state slugs, hairline rules), p.11 (voice), p.7 (copper discipline). Current shell: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx (HomeMapHero + rails) and shell.css (token-driven, 600+ lines). The hero must be architected WITH BB-098: the same MapLibre canvas instance survives the hero-to-explore transition — see that bead for the architecture options before fixing the hero DOM structure.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Homepage hero renders the live map as the dominant element with layered text passing AA on the dark basemap. 2. All public routes render on the new shell — no page retains the old white-card/zero-radius look. 3. Copper audit: orientation moments only, roughly 10-15% presence. 4. Type scale matches guide p.8; dark theme first-class; both themes verified in-browser with screenshots. 5. Voice pass applied to shell/nav/hero copy per guide p.11. 6. Reduced motion verified; all existing a11y tests pass.
notes:
Session 2026-07-18 verification pass (in-browser, apps/web dev server, both themes, desktop + 375px):
- CONFIRMED: homepage hero renders the live national map full-bleed with "History, pinned to place." / "People. Places. Evidence. Context." voice copy layered over it, readable in both themes (screenshots taken).
- CONFIRMED: dark theme (default) and light "Theme: Light" toggle both render correctly; map plate + on-map header/footer intentionally stay dark in light theme (theme-invariant surfaces per design-direction-v4 §6) - this is deliberate, not a bug.
- CONFIRMED: copper reserved for orientation (active nav underline, CTA fill, cluster/point bubbles, selected states) - no stray copper found outside those roles in the surfaces checked. Full pixel-composition % audit still needs BB-101's scripted sweep.
- CONFIRMED: all public routes reachable from nav render on the new shell (spot-checked via nav links); no legacy white-card look observed.
- PARTIAL: reduced-motion verified at the SOURCE level only this session (camera-presets.ts's prefersReducedMotion()/REDUCED_MOTION_CAMERA_PRESETS reviewed, correct) - did not force-emulate prefers-reduced-motion in a live browser this pass.
- NOT DONE THIS SESSION: full a11y test suite pass (contrast.test.ts already covered by existing tests; broader ARIA/keyboard sweep is BB-101 scope).
- Found and cleared a one-time stale .next dev-cache artifact (ReferenceError on first cold compile after the big bb->blap rename) that made the hero look broken in a screenshot; root cause was NOT a source bug, confirmed by a from-clean rebuild + fresh tab reproducing correctly. No code change needed for that; flagging in case it recurs for another dev.
- Cleanup: removed 3 confirmed-dead pre-ADR-017 files (apps/web/src/app/map/MapLibreCanvas.tsx, dark-archive-style.ts, map-source.seed.json) - unused since the (map) route-group rewrite, verified via grep + tsc --noEmit (0 new errors; same 5 pre-existing errors as before, all outside map code).
Explore map side panels: compact filter/toggle controls (sm radius, coherent outline register), quieter result-list selection (copper left rule + subtle fill), tighter metadata grid.

metadata.modelRouting: opus for design direction; sonnet for buildout; haiku for long-tail reskins

title: BB-096: Brand pack v3 adoption: tokens, type, radius, and mark reconciliation
description:
Expansion bead BB-096 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief (owner voice memo). Brand pack v3 was committed as brand-system/ in 68b3373; filenames lowercased, internal manifest/checksum references fixed, and web-served copies staged at apps/web/public/brand/ on branch redesign/brand-map-beads (this bead's precursor work, already done).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + brand-system/guide/black-book-brand-guide-final.pdf (14 pp, "Black Book Brand Identity System 3.0.0-final")
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-067, BB-068 (closed — this bead supersedes their token/type decisions wherever the final pack differs)

Why: the final brand pack and the shipped packages/ui tokens disagree on four load-bearing points. The site currently ships pure-white canvas (#FFFFFF), Sora display type, zero border radius, and a code-generated logo lockup (BlackBookLogo.tsx renders the wordmark as text beside the symbol). The final pack specifies Archive Paper canvas #F4EFE5 (+ Surface #FBF8F2), Inter Display + Inter, soft radii (8/16/28px), and an outlined-artwork lockup with the explicit misuse rule "Do not recreate it by typing the full name beside the symbol" — the current header and OG image violate that rule. Every other redesign bead builds on this foundation; it is the root of the tranche.

Deliver:
- Tokens v3 in packages/ui/src/tokens/ + styles/tokens.css: Archive Paper #F4EFE5 canvas, Surface #FBF8F2, Charcoal #161616, Stone #6D675F (secondary text, 4.9:1 on paper), Rule #D7D0C4 (hairlines), Copper Pin #B86B2A (graphic-scale only), copper text #8E4F2A on light (5.5:1) / #D07A32 on dark (6.1:1), Page Sand #D8A178. Dark theme stays first-class: paper/ink inversion per brand-system/tokens/black-book-brand.css.
- Radius tokens 8/16/28px (sm/md/lg) replacing the zero-radius rule; record the supersession in docs/ui/brand.md (BB-007's flat/zero-radius language is superseded by the final pack; bevels/shadows/gradients/glows remain banned everywhere).
- Typography: Inter Display (600-700) replaces Sora for display/headlines; Inter 400-600 for UI; Source Serif 4 400-600 editorial; IBM Plex Mono 400-500 data/citations. Fluid scale from guide p.8: display 48-72, H1 36-48, H2 24-32, body 16-18. Update next/font loading in apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx; everything stays free/open-source.
- Mark: the official artwork SVGs (apps/web/public/brand/black-book-primary-{light,dark}.svg + mark/compact variants) become the rendered source of truth for SiteHeader, admin masthead, favicon, and OG image; retire or refactor BlackBookLogo.tsx so no surface reconstructs the lockup by typing the name beside the symbol. Favicon route uses the pack favicon set (favicon.svg + PNG sizes); OG uses the pack OG images or regenerates from the primary artwork.
- Copper discipline encoded in docs: copper is roughly 10-15% of any composition, reserved for the moment of orientation (active locations, selected filters, primary actions, evidence markers) — guidance in docs/ui/brand.md plus a review checklist item, not a hard token.
- docs/ui/brand.md rewritten against the final pack; docs/ui/brand-reference/ old-package PNGs replaced or clearly marked superseded; stale packages/ui/src/brand/assets/bb-logo-*.svg retired.
- Contrast unit tests extended to every new token pair (guide p.7 accessible-pairs table is the source: Ink/Paper 17.3:1, #8E4F2A/Paper 5.5:1, #D07A32/Ink 6.1:1, Stone/Paper 4.9:1).

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No surface types a second B or renders the product name as text beside the symbol.
- No bevels, shadows, gradients, glows, rotation, or recoloring of the mark (approved light/dark/monochrome variants only).
- Raw Copper Pin #B86B2A never carries body-size text on the light canvas.
- Clear space around any lockup >= the visible pin width.

Model routing (guidance): sonnet-class default for token/asset/doc work; haiku-class for mechanical file wiring and reference sweeps; opus-class not needed — the contrast test suite plus the guide p.7 table make correctness mechanically checkable.
design: Source of truth: brand-system/ (3.0.0-final, checksummed; filenames lowercased 2026-07-17) + guide PDF p.7 (color system and accessible pairs), p.8 (type scale), p.13 (theme tokens and asset-selection table). Web-served copies staged at apps/web/public/brand/. Conflicts found in the 2026-07-17 review: white vs Archive Paper canvas; Sora vs Inter Display; zero radius vs 8/16/28; typed wordmark vs outlined-artwork lockup (guide misuse rule: do not recreate the lockup by typing the full name beside the symbol). Guide voice for color: Black and paper lead, copper points — copper is a navigational signal, not a decorative wash (10-15% of composition).
acceptance_criteria: 1. packages/ui tokens + styles/tokens.css match guide p.7/p.13 values exactly; contrast tests cover every new pair and pass. 2. Inter Display loaded and applied to all display/headline surfaces; Sora removed. 3. Radius tokens 8/16/28 applied across components; supersession of the zero-radius rule recorded in docs/ui/brand.md. 4. Header, admin masthead, favicon, and OG image render from the official artwork assets; no typed-wordmark reconstruction remains (grep-verifiable). 5. docs/ui/brand.md rewritten against the final pack; stale assets retired. 6. pnpm ui + web tests and workspace typecheck clean.
close_reason: Tokens v3, Inter Display via opsz, radii 8/16/28, artwork lockup on header/masthead/favicon/OG, docs rewritten, contrast suite extended (28/28 ui tests). Caveat: workspace web typecheck currently red from an UNRELATED concurrent entity-media stream (packages/firebase); BB-096 files verified not the cause via stash-revert. Re-verify at BB-101 gate. Committed 6fdec13.

metadata.modelRouting: sonnet default; haiku for mechanical wiring; opus not needed

title: BB-095: Sensitivity presentation, present-day advisories, and the disclaimer framework
description:
Expansion bead BB-095 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (sensitivity presentation, present-day advisories, disclaimer framework).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — advisories & disclaimers) (include notable people whose actions are morally flaggable while shielding the product from endorsement; a place can be historically significant and "dangerous today"; site-wide and per-class disclaimers)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-015, BB-019, BB-069, BB-090

Why: three distinct problems share one presentation-and-posture layer: (a) notable-but-flaggable people must be INCLUDED — an index of history cannot omit them — while the product never reads as endorsing them; (b) a place can be historically vital and currently inadvisable to visit (private property, site lost, active advisory) — a present-day fact that must inform presentation and, per the standing crime-stats rule, must NEVER touch scoring; (c) the site needs one coherent disclaimer framework (educational purpose, not legal/travel advice, per-class notices, non-endorsement) instead of ad-hoc banners. One bead because all three render through the same banner/disclaimer components and the same legal posture doc — splitting them creates three agents fighting over one component path. Extends the BB-090 sensitivity schema, the BB-082 language discipline, BB-069 voice, and docs/security/ugc-legal-posture.md. P0 because a public beta without disclaimers is a legal exposure: this bead gates BB-063 (beta gate), not the surface beads (BB-051/052 carry a minimal inline fallback).

Deliver:
- Advisory record model on place-kind entities ({advisoryClass: private_property | access_restricted | site_lost | verify_before_travel | official_travel_advisory, sourcedClaimIds, asOf, reviewCadence}). Advisories are CLAIMS — citations required (property records, an NAACP travel advisory as a dated sourced claim, news of demolition). Presentation-layer only: banner + muted map treatment; a projection/scoring test extending the BB-082 crime-stats discipline proves advisory fields never enter any scoring input or composite.
- Sensitivity presentation for BB-090-flagged entities: shared context-banner and non-endorsement components on entity pages, map cards, and search results; include-with-context is the rule — a flag never suppresses an entity that passes notability; copy passes constitution language caps (procedural, evidence-capped, no celebratory framing for flagged subjects).
- Disclaimer framework: a single versioned registry of disclaimer texts keyed by class — site-wide (educational purpose, accuracy limits, not legal or travel advice), per-class (visiting historic sites, private property, sensitive/violent content), non-endorsement (flagged individuals), safety advisory — rendered only through shared components (no ad-hoc disclaimer strings in app code), each with a review date, wired into docs/security/ugc-legal-posture.md and exposed to the BB-088 trust surfaces.
- "Dangerous today" never appears as copy or classification: advisories use procedural, dated, sourced language; no red, no danger iconography (dignity rules).
- Delineation doc: sensitivity flags vs BB-015 living-person compliance vs BB-082 historical place conditions — three lanes, never conflated.

Stress-test addition (2026-07-17): sensitivity flags apply to documented CONDUCT only, never to identity attributes (sexual orientation, disability, religion, immigration status, etc.) — a reviewer applying a BB-090 sensitivity flag must cite a conduct-based rationale (an action, a documented decision, a legal outcome), never a personal characteristic. This is a non-goal stated explicitly because historical figures whose marginalized identity was itself treated as scandalous by their contemporaries (e.g. a closeted gay organizer) must never have that identity re-flagged by this product as if it were the moral concern.
design: Merged flags+advisories+disclaimers into one bead: they share components and legal posture; three thin beads would violate exclusive-path ownership on the banner/disclaimer component tree. Advisories are sourced claims with an asOf date, not editorial labels — the same evidentiary bar as everything else, and the only honest way to publish "currently inadvisable". Advisory data lives outside scoring by construction and by test, extending the standing crime-stats rule (an advisory that fed a score would rebuild the redlining machine BB-082 was designed to prevent). Include-with-context over exclusion for flagged people: the shield is framing (non-endorsement, procedural language), not omission — omission would be a silent editorial judgment the methodology page could not defend. Rejected: "dangerous today" as a status or badge (unsourceable editorializing; replaced by dated, cited advisory claims); folding disclaimers into BB-088 (BB-088 is the trust/pre-bunking voice, P1 and later — disclaimers gate the beta); per-surface hand-written banners (drift; single registry instead).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Advisory record model on place-kind entities ({advisoryClass: private_property|access_restricted|site_lost|verify_before_travel|official_travel_advisory, sourcedClaimIds, asOf, reviewCadence}) with citations required on every advisory; a projection/scoring test extending the BB-082 crime-stats discipline proves advisory fields are absent from every scoring input and composite. 2. Shared context-banner and non-endorsement components render for BB-090-flagged entities on entity pages, map cards, and search results; flags never suppress an entity that passes notability; copy passes the constitution's procedural language caps. 3. A single versioned disclaimer registry keyed by class (site-wide educational/accuracy/not-legal-or-travel-advice; visiting historic sites; private property; sensitive content; non-endorsement; safety advisory) renders only through shared components — a repo check finds no ad-hoc disclaimer strings in app code; every disclaimer carries a review date. 4. "Dangerous today" appears nowhere as copy or classification: present-day advisories render as dated, cited, procedural statements with muted map treatment (no red, no danger iconography). 5. The framework is wired into docs/security/ugc-legal-posture.md and exposed to the BB-088 trust surfaces; the BB-063 launch checklist gains a verifiable condition that all public surfaces render the framework. 6. A delineation doc separates sensitivity flags, BB-015 living-person compliance, and BB-082 historical place conditions into three lanes with owning beads named. 7. A reviewer applying a sensitivity flag must record a conduct-based rationale (citing an action, decision, or documented outcome); a repo/documentation check states identity attributes are never a valid rationale on their own.
close_reason: Sensitivity presentation, present-day advisories, and disclaimer registry landed. Advisory model (packages/domain/src/advisory.ts): advisoryClass enum, sourced/dated PlaceAdvisoryRecord, procedural-language-only enforcement (assertProceduralAdvisoryLanguage bans danger/urgency framing), and assertAdvisoryAbsentFromScoringInput extending the standing crime-stats-never-scores rule with a real poisoned-input test. Disclaimer registry (packages/domain/src/disclaimers.ts): versioned DISCLAIMER_REGISTRY keyed by class, identity-attribute-framing ban. Shared banner components (apps/web/src/components/{AdvisoryNotice,DisclaimerBanner,SensitivityContextBanner}.tsx) — include-with-context, never suppression, verified by an SSR markup test suite. Fixed two real bugs surfaced by actually running the tests (not just trusting the self-report): a stray .tsx import extension violating this repo's TS config, and an overly broad /hidden/i suppression-check regex that false-positived on legitimate aria-hidden/bb-visually-hidden accessibility markup — replaced with a token-boundary regex that still catches real suppression (display:none, a literal hidden attribute) without matching accessibility conventions. Also fixed a genuine cross-package JSX-transform gap: packages/ui/src/components/Notice.tsx (a pre-existing shared component) had no explicit React import, which is fine under its own package's automatic JSX runtime but breaks when apps/web's Next.js jsx:preserve config causes the test loader to classic-transform it — added a defensive React import, verified packages/ui's own suite still passes (26/26) so the fix is safe both ways. 'Dangerous today' framing confirmed absent from both source and rendered HTML output via a real grep-the-render test. domain 607/607, web 62/62.

title: BB-092: History graph substrate: edge vocabulary, containment chains, and derived all-time/decade views
description:
Expansion bead BB-092 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (history graph substrate: edge vocabulary and derived views).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — entity ontology) ("a solid graph/dependency map of entities flowing through history — an all-time view and decade-by-decade views")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-019, BB-090, BB-091

Why: the graph is an EXTENSION, not an invention — EntityRelationship already exists in @black-book/domain as directed, typed, evidence-required edges (assertRelationshipHasEvidence) with TemporalContext, and entityRelationships is a registered collection. What is missing: the historical-causation edge vocabulary (caused/enabled/influenced...), containment-chain materialization (the owner's anchor-on-state rule), and any way for the public product to SEE the graph — the public projection today carries zero relationships, which makes BB-052's existing "Related people, places..." and "Timelines" deliverables unimplementable. Firestore has no graph queries, so views are derived at publish time into the immutable release, exactly the BB-019 snapshot discipline — no graph DB, no request-time traversal.

Deliver:
- Extend the EntityRelationship type vocabulary with historical-causation edges: caused | enabled | influenced | participated_in | overturned | commemorates (alongside existing located_at, founded, member_of, part_of, successor_of, governed_by, cites...). Each new type documents direction and temporal semantics; every edge remains a claim with citations; causal edges require TemporalContext.
- Containment-chain materialization: located_at/part_of chains (spot -> city -> county -> state) resolved at projection build into a denormalized containment path with BB-091 jurisdiction ids on every geo-anchored entity — "anchor most things around state" becomes a queryable field, with lat/lng+radius as the rendering attribute, not the identity.
- Derived graph views as release artifacts (BB-019 pattern): publicReleases/{releaseId}/graph docs — per-entity bounded adjacency (typed, time-scoped, evidence-count-ordered, capped top-N) + per-decade node/edge sets computed with BB-090 era buckets + an all-time union view. Build is deterministic, cycle-safe, bounded-depth, re-runnable in the publication worker.
- Firestore-native storage: edges stay collection docs; composite indexes on (from, type), (to, type), (type, validFrom); documented read-cost bounds. No graph database.
- Public projection gains typed related entries ({id, type, direction, timespan}) consumed by BB-052 related/timeline sections and the BB-093 surface.
- Edge intake through the existing operator CLI / research-case pipeline (extends the BB-085-landed commands — no parallel writer); graph fixtures added to the gold corpus so BB-047 regression-tests the build.

Stress-test amendment (2026-07-17, entity-ontology stress test): extends the edge vocabulary with authored (person/organization -> publication/artifact creation attribution, distinct from founded which is reserved for orgs/institutions) and adds an optional role qualifier (organizer|speaker|participant) on the existing attended edge so leadership/organizing roles are distinguishable from rank-and-file attendance. Graph-materialization reconciliation: derived graph views also treat BB-086 FactRecord subjects[] entries as an implicit edge source (or mirror key subjects[] links into EntityRelationship at publish time) — without this, entities linked to a fact only via subjects[] (e.g. a person who is the subject of an event record, with no separate formal relationship) would be invisible to the browse graph despite being genuinely connected. Edge-type usage guardrail: caused and enabled are reserved for consensus, systemic historical causation backed by citable secondary-source evidence (e.g. HOLC redlining caused disinvestment); contested or single-incident causal claims (e.g. whether a specific statute enabled a specific act of violence) route through the existing cites edge instead, never an asserted causal edge — this keeps the graph from asserting contested conclusions as fact. Decade-view bucketing uses active span, not creation date: an entity with an open-ended or ongoing active period (an org, institution, or movement) appears in every decade it was active, derived from status/statusHistory and start/end fields, not merely the decade it was founded. Succession-chain non-leakage: when a historically-designated place is superseded (annexed/renamed), its historical statusHistory/condition record stays anchored to the historical entity; queries against the modern successor via successor_of surface the designation as linked historical context, never as the successor's current status.
design:
Derived-at-publish over request-time traversal: Firestore traversal at request time is unbounded reads and unbounded cost; the release-snapshot discipline already exists (BB-019) and graph views are just another published artifact. No graph DB: violates Firebase/GCP-native + free-first, and at this scale composite indexes + precomputed views are sufficient — recorded with an escalation trigger if traversal depth requirements ever exceed the bounded build. Bounded top-N adjacency caps doc sizes and forces editorial ranking by evidence, not volume. Decade views precomputed because the decade question is the product's signature query, and computing it per-request re-derives the same buckets forever. Edge vocabulary kept to six additions — every type must have distinct direction/temporal semantics; rejected a large speculative vocabulary (edges without semantics become related_to mush). Rejected: storing the graph in the entity doc (unbounded doc growth); relationship edges without evidence (violates the existing assert).

Edge vocabulary stays additive and narrow per the original six-type discipline: authored and the attended role qualifier were added because no existing type could express creation attribution or organizer-vs-attendee weight without stretching semantics (a stress test against real cases — MLK authoring 'I Have a Dream', A. Philip Randolph organizing the March on Washington — surfaced both gaps concretely). subjects[]-as-graph-input closes a real blind spot: EntityRelationship and FactRecord.subjects[] are two different collections, and a graph view built from EntityRelationship alone would silently omit any entity connected to a fact only through subjects[] (e.g. Trayvon Martin's connection to the event record of his death, which is not modeled as a formal typed relationship anywhere else). The caused/enabled-vs-cites guardrail exists because the graph must never assert a contested individual causal claim (a law 'enabling' a specific killing) with the same directed-edge confidence as a settled systemic claim (a policy causing measurable disinvestment) — cites lets the connection exist without asserting the contested conclusion.
acceptance_criteria: 1. EntityRelationship vocabulary extends with caused|enabled|influenced|participated_in|overturned|commemorates; each new type documents direction and temporal semantics; assertRelationshipHasEvidence still holds for every edge and causal edges require a TemporalContext. 2. Containment chains (located_at/part_of: spot->city->county->state) are materialized at projection build into a denormalized containment path with BB-091 jurisdiction ids on every geo-anchored entity. 3. Derived graph views ship as release artifacts in the BB-019 immutable-release pattern: per-entity bounded adjacency docs (typed, time-scoped, evidence-count-ordered, capped top-N), per-decade node/edge sets computed with BB-090 era buckets, and an all-time union view; the build is deterministic, cycle-safe, bounded-depth, and re-runnable. 4. No graph database: edges remain Firestore collection docs with composite indexes on (from,type), (to,type), (type,validFrom); read-cost bounds documented. 5. The public projection gains typed related entries ({id, type, direction, timespan}) sufficient for BB-052 related-entity and timeline sections; nothing numeric beyond evidence counts appears publicly. 6. Edge intake flows through the existing operator CLI/research-case pipeline (no parallel writer); gold-corpus graph fixtures added so BB-047 regression-tests the build. 7. The EntityRelationship vocabulary gains authored (person/organization -> publication/artifact) and an optional role qualifier (organizer|speaker|participant) on attended; both documented with direction/temporal semantics and evidence-required like every other edge. 8. Derived graph-view materialization also ingests BB-086 FactRecord subjects[] entries as an edge source (directly or via publish-time mirroring into EntityRelationship), so entities connected to a fact only through subjects[] are visible in the published graph and not silently dropped. 9. A documented guardrail restricts caused/enabled to consensus, citable systemic causation; contested or single-incident causal claims use cites instead — reviewed at intake, not left to submitter judgment. 10. Per-decade view materialization buckets an entity into every decade of its active span (derived from status/statusHistory and start/end fields), not only the decade of its creation/founding date — a test proves an entity founded in one decade and still active appears in every subsequent published decade view. 11. A test proves succession chains (successor_of) do not leak a superseded entity's historical statusHistory/condition designation onto the successor entity as current status; the designation surfaces only as linked historical context.
close_reason: History graph substrate landed: EntityRelationship extended with caused/enabled/influenced/participated_in/overturned/commemorates/authored plus an attended role qualifier (packages/domain/src/relationship.ts); containment-chain materialization, per-entity/per-decade/all-time derived graph release views, BB-086 subjects[] mirroring, and succession-chain non-leakage in packages/domain/src/graph/ (new module). Three critical invariants (acceptance criteria 9/10/11 — causal guardrail, active-span decade bucketing, succession non-leakage) independently re-verified by reading the actual test assertions, not just trusting self-report: all three are genuinely and rigorously tested against the exact scenarios named in the bead (HOLC-redlining allowed, statute-enabled-killing rejected; 1950s-founded-still-active entity spans 1950s-2020s; annexed place's historic status never leaks onto its modern successor). Public projection gained typed related entries (apps/web/src/data/public-seed.ts, packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts). Edge intake wired end-to-end through a new operator-cli propose-edge command (packages/operator-cli/src/edge-intake.ts + cli.ts), reusing the existing BB-029 quarantine pipeline, no parallel writer. domain 607/607, firebase 142/142, operator-cli 57/57.

title: BB-091: Jurisdiction registry and geographic reference substrate
description:
Expansion bead BB-091 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (jurisdiction registry and geographic reference substrate).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — entity ontology) ("anchor most things around state ... a law: federal, state, county ... do we store all zips? all states, counties, cities?" + "as close to the known location as possible")
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-037, BB-070

Why: Jurisdiction is the biggest storage gap in the repo — it exists as a domain type (geography/location.ts, with country|state|county|city levels and validFrom/validTo) but has NO collection, no docs, no index; LawFields.jurisdictionId and EntityLocation.jurisdictionIds are dangling string refs. BB-087 cannot model law effect-areas, BB-082 cannot bind area conditions, and the map cannot aggregate by county without it. Counties are the unit of the historical record (sundown towns, county-scoped ordinances, county splits). This bead instantiates the EXISTING type into storage and adds the precision-radius policy the owner asked for — it extends geography/precision.ts and redaction.ts, never replaces them. All data free and public domain (U.S. Census); runs-itself (static reference data, at most annual refresh).

Deliver:
- jurisdictions/{id} Firestore collection instantiating the domain Jurisdiction type: 51 state docs sourced from the existing us-geography module (single source of truth, no duplicate table) + all ~3,143 county docs with FIPS, name, state, bbox, centroid from Census TIGER/Gazetteer files (public domain); idempotent load/refresh script; registered in paths.ts and the data-access registry; rules public-read/admin-write; composite indexes.
- Scoping decision recorded as ADR: states + counties wholesale (small, essential); cities/places ON-DEMAND only (doc created at first entity/geocode reference via Census place FIPS — ~19k incorporated places are churny and mostly unreferenced); ZIPs NEVER stored as reference data (postal artifacts, historically unstable, ~42k; ZIP stays modern-input-only per the existing guard in location.ts). Historical county boundary changes NOT exhaustively modeled: validFrom/validTo fields are ready, records added on-demand when an entity needs one (Newberry Atlas of Historical County Boundaries noted as the future source).
- Precision-radius policy in domain geography: geoPrecision tier vocabulary (exact-site | block | locality | county | state — ONE definition shared with the BB-086 spec) maps deterministically to display radii (fixed radii for site/block; locality/county/state radii derived from jurisdiction bbox), stored per EntityLocation with precisionBasis (source-documented | geocoded | approximated | redacted-by-rule). Bulk-loaded historic sites default to best-documented precision; redaction remains the EXCEPTION applied only by rule (BB-015 living-person residences etc.) — redactLocationForPublic and PRECISION_RANK stay authoritative and fail closed.
- LawFields.jurisdictionId and EntityLocation.jurisdictionIds become resolvable references; projection build fails closed on dangling refs. Map geometry for jurisdiction-scoped records (laws, area conditions) derives from jurisdiction reference data, never a point.
- County polygons (Census cartographic boundary files 1:500k, public domain) compiled into the BB-070 map-platform tile assets (PMTiles); Firestore stores bbox/centroid only, keeping docs small.
design: Counties yes / cities on-demand / ZIPs never: counties are the unit of jurisdiction and of the historical record, and 3,143 small docs are trivial; cities are churny and mostly unreferenced; ZIPs are postal artifacts with no historical validity and the domain already guards them as modern-input-only. Bbox/centroid in Firestore + polygons in tiles: Firestore is the wrong home for polygon geometry (doc size, read cost) and BB-070 already ships a PMTiles pipeline. Radius is derived from precision tier, not hand-stored per entity: deterministic, auditable, and reconcilable with redaction (PRECISION_RANK compares tiers, not meters). Rejected: exhaustive historical county reconstruction (a research project, not launch substrate — fields ready, records on-demand); a paid geocoding/boundary API (free Census data covers it); storing radius as a free number per entity (drifts, ungovernable).
acceptance_criteria: 1. jurisdictions collection exists (registered in paths.ts and the data-access registry; rules public-read/admin-write; composite indexes committed) instantiating the domain Jurisdiction type: 51 state docs sourced from the existing us-geography module plus ~3,143 county docs with FIPS, name, state, bbox, centroid from U.S. Census TIGER/Gazetteer files (public domain), loaded by an idempotent script. 2. City/place jurisdiction docs are created on-demand only at first entity/geocode reference (Census place FIPS); an ADR records the scoping decision: states+counties wholesale, cities on-demand, ZIPs never stored as reference data (ZIP remains modern input-only per the existing guard); historical county boundary records are on-demand with validFrom/validTo support. 3. Precision-radius policy lands in domain geography: the geoPrecision tier vocabulary (exact-site|block|locality|county|state, one definition shared with the BB-086 spec) maps deterministically to display radii (fixed for site/block, bbox-derived for locality/county/state), stored per EntityLocation with precisionBasis (source-documented|geocoded|approximated|redacted-by-rule). 4. Redaction stays the exception: redactLocationForPublic and PRECISION_RANK remain authoritative and fail closed; a test proves bulk records default to best-documented precision and only rule-triggered records coarsen. 5. LawFields.jurisdictionId and EntityLocation.jurisdictionIds resolve to jurisdiction docs; the projection build fails closed on dangling refs. 6. County polygons (Census cartographic boundary 1:500k) are compiled into the BB-070 map-platform tile assets; Firestore stores bbox/centroid only. 7. All sources registered through the BB-037 registry with license verdicts (Census = public domain); no paid geo APIs.
close_reason: Jurisdiction domain type instantiated into Firestore: jurisdictions/{id} collection (paths.ts + data-access registry + public-read rules + composite indexes), idempotent Census TIGER/Gazetteer state+county loader (packages/firebase/src/jurisdictions/), geoPrecision tier vocabulary + deterministic display-radius mapping + precisionBasis in packages/domain/src/geography/precision.ts (registered through the BB-037 census-geo adapter with a public-domain license verdict), fail-closed dangling-jurisdiction-reference gate (jurisdiction-refs.ts), EntityLocation.geoPrecisionTier/precisionBasis fields wired. ADR-016 records scoping decisions (states+counties wholesale, cities on-demand, ZIPs never) plus the human cloud-apply checklist (rules/index deploy, real Census file + Firestore run — none executed live, per no-live-apply policy). redaction.ts/PRECISION_RANK untouched. domain 499/499, firebase 142/142, data-access 18/18, all typechecks clean.

title: BB-090: Entity ontology: kind-specific status lifecycle, notability basis, and era model
description:
Expansion bead BB-090 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (entity ontology: status lifecycle, notability basis, era model).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — entity ontology) ("an index of Black history in the U.S. ... active / historic / inactive; deceased for people ... not all rappers who are Black, but icons, firsts, hall-of-famers")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-017, BB-040

Why: everything in the product now hangs off a first-class Entity, and the substrate mostly exists — CanonicalEntity (11 kinds, aliases, identifiers, livingStatus, mergeState) and per-kind field bags already live in @black-book/domain. What is genuinely absent: an entity-level status lifecycle (only mergeState exists), an auditable inclusion rubric (inclusion is emergent from relevance+confidence gates, invisible to readers), and a structured era/decade model (era is a free string in the web seed; decades are computed only inside the embeddings package). This bead EXTENDS entity.ts and the relevance-gate machinery rather than building a parallel ontology, and becomes the single vocabulary source the BB-086 FactRecord spec, BB-087 law badges, and BB-072 era pre-filters all import.

Deliver:
- Kind-specific EntityStatus vocabularies extending CanonicalEntity: place/school/organization/institution: active | historic | inactive; law: in_force | amended | repealed | struck_down | enjoined (the exact vocabulary BB-087 badges already use — defined once, imported there); person: status DERIVES from existing livingStatus (no second field, unknown => treated as living per BB-015); event: no status field — its when-span is authoritative (an event is not "inactive").
- Time-scoped statusHistory[] designation records ({status, validFrom?, validTo?, datePrecision, basisClaimIds[]}) so point-in-time questions ("what was true in 1930") are answerable; current status derived from the open-ended record, never hand-edited separately. This is the storage pattern BB-082 sundown designations use (possible/probable/surely stays claim confidence, never a boolean).
- notabilityBasis[] structured inclusion records ({criterion from a per-kind enum: first-to-do-X | major_honor_or_hall_of_fame | landmark_or_national_register | court_precedent | movement_significance | documented_site | community_anchor | only_or_oldest, note, evidenceIds[]}); >=1 basis required to publish, wired as an additional relevance gate (extends the 7 gates, never replaces); per-kind rubric text authored for the BB-088 methodology definitions section — this is the "icons, firsts, hall-of-famers" filter made auditable. Initial cultural-figure calibration ships as "icons & firsts only" (hall-of-fame inductions, major national honors, documented firsts, movement significance; commercial milestones like certifications never qualify alone) — reviewable rubric text, ratify or widen before BB-094 auto-derives notability from it.
- Shared era model: datePrecision vocabulary (day | month | year | decade | circa, optional range) defined once in domain (the BB-086 spec imports it); deriveEraBuckets maps any span to every overlapping decade (1948-1972 => 1940s..1970s), replacing the embeddings-local deriveEraBucket.
- Entity-level sensitivity flag SCHEMA ({class: contested_legacy | perpetrator_associated | violence_associated | enslaver_or_segregationist, note, basisClaimIds[]}) — schema only; presentation/disclaimers are BB-095; explicitly distinct from BB-015 living-person compliance and from location sensitivity classes.
- Zod mirrors in packages/firebase firestore types; public projection additions (status, eraBuckets, notability criterion labels, sensitivity class) — all non-numeric; seed PublicEntityView reconciled; ADR recording the ontology decisions.

Stress-test amendment (2026-07-17, entity-ontology stress test): adds a 12th EntityKind, movement (Civil Rights Movement, Great Migration, Black Power, Black Arts Movement, etc. — the connective, multi-decade, multi-actor phenomena that individual events/organizations are part_of), with its own field bag ({startYear, endYear|ongoing, keyOrganizationIds[], keyPersonIds[], regionJurisdictionIds[] referencing BB-091 jurisdictions, summary}); movement status vocabulary is active|historic (no inactive — a movement doesn't pause, it concludes); movement_significance was already a notabilityBasis criterion, now explicitly applicable to movement-kind entities themselves, not just people associated with one. Scope clarification: statusHistory[] is entity-LIFECYCLE status only (active/historic/inactive/in_force/etc per kind) — area/condition designations (sundown-town, redlining grade, exclusion infrastructure) are NOT statusHistory records; they remain BB-082's own time-scoped, evidence-backed layer records, following the same pattern but a distinct field, never merged into the generic entity status array.
design:
Kind-specific status vocabularies over one global enum (a person is never "inactive"; forcing one enum makes every kind lie). Person status derives from livingStatus — a second source of truth would drift against the BB-015 compliance lane. Status is a history of time-scoped designation records, not a scalar — the decade views and BB-082 sundown modeling both need "status as of decade D", and a scalar cannot answer it. Notability is an auditable basis record, never a score — numeric scores are banned from public payloads by standing rule, and a visible criterion ("National Register listing", "first Black justice") is the defensible answer to "why is X in and Y out". Era buckets are derived from dates, not stored authoritative — one derivation function, consumed everywhere. Cultural-figure notability starts at "icons & firsts only" (hall-of-fame/major honors/documented firsts/movement significance; commercial success alone never qualifies) per owner calibration — ships as reviewable rubric text, ratified before BB-094 leans on it. Rejected: separate NotabilityScore (banned + gameable); global status enum; putting the ontology in a new package (extends entity.ts in place — reuse over parallel).

Stress-test addition: movement is a distinct entity kind rather than a long-duration event, because an event is a discrete happening and a movement is a sustained, multi-actor, multi-decade phenomenon that individual events/organizations resolve into via part_of — conflating the two would blur exactly the distinction the decade/all-time graph view exists to show. statusHistory stays scoped to entity lifecycle only; overloading it with area-condition designations (sundown/redlining) was flagged as a bad implicit merge in the BB-082 amendment language and is corrected here and in BB-082.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Kind-specific EntityStatus vocabularies land in @black-book/domain extending CanonicalEntity (place/school/organization/institution: active|historic|inactive; law: in_force|amended|repealed|struck_down|enjoined — the same vocabulary BB-087 badges import; person status derives from existing livingStatus with no second field; event carries no status), with Zod mirrors in packages/firebase types. 2. Time-scoped statusHistory designation records ({status, validFrom?, validTo?, datePrecision, basisClaimIds[]}) support point-in-time queries — a test answers "what was this entity's status in decade D"; current status derives from the open-ended record and is never independently editable. 3. notabilityBasis[] records ({criterion from a per-kind enum, note, evidenceIds[]}) are required for publication: the projection build fails closed on a publishable entity with zero basis entries, wired as an additional gate alongside the existing relevance gates (extends, never replaces). 4. The per-kind notability rubric (firsts, major honors/halls of fame, landmark/National Register designation, court precedent, movement significance, documented site, community anchor, only/oldest) is committed as reviewable rubric text destined for the BB-088 methodology definitions section, with the initial cultural-figure calibration set to icons-and-firsts-only (commercial milestones never qualify alone) pending owner ratification. 5. Shared era model: datePrecision vocabulary (day|month|year|decade|circa, optional range) defined once in domain and consumed by the BB-086 FactRecord spec; deriveEraBuckets maps any span to every overlapping decade (1948-1972 => 1940s,1950s,1960s,1970s) and replaces the embeddings-local deriveEraBucket. 6. Entity-level sensitivity flag schema ({class, note, basisClaimIds[]}) lands schema-only (presentation is BB-095), documented as distinct from BB-015 living-person compliance and from location sensitivity classes. 7. Public projection additions (status, eraBuckets, notability criterion labels, sensitivity class) are non-numeric — a test proves no numeric score appears in any public payload; an ADR records the ontology decisions and rejected alternatives. 8. A 12th EntityKind, movement, lands in @black-book/domain with its own field bag (startYear, endYear|ongoing, keyOrganizationIds[], keyPersonIds[], regionJurisdictionIds[] referencing BB-091 jurisdictions, summary) and status vocabulary active|historic; movement-kind entities can carry notabilityBasis records including movement_significance. 9. statusHistory[] is documented and enforced as entity-lifecycle status only; a design note and code comment make explicit that area/condition designations (sundown-town, redlining grade, exclusion infrastructure) are never stored in statusHistory — they remain BB-082's own layer records.
close_reason: Kind-specific EntityStatus vocabularies, time-scoped statusHistory[], notabilityBasis[] wired as an 8th relevance gate (numeric-score-ban test passing), shared datePrecision/deriveEraBuckets model (replaces embeddings-local duplicate), schema-only sensitivity flag, and the 12th movement EntityKind all landed in packages/domain/src/{entity.ts,entity-kinds.ts,entity-status.ts,era.ts,movement.ts,relevance/notability-gate.ts}. Zod mirrors in packages/firebase, non-numeric public projection fields in apps/web seed. School.ts statusHistory naming collision resolved (renamed to milestones, old alias deprecated). ADR-015 records the decisions. domain 499/499, firebase 142/142 (+8 pre-existing emulator skips), web tests/typecheck all clean.

title: BB-089: Pre-launch resilience and operator protection
description:
Expansion bead BB-089 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (pre-launch resilience & operator protection: the perimeter delta).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("compile the dream team when it comes to protecting this application... protect it without saying that is what we are doing explicitly") + adversarial red-team pass 2026-07-17
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-078

Why: a solo-run, deliberately controversial archive will face coordinated NON-technical attack. The existing threat model (BB-004) is strong on technical integrity, but the red-team pass found the highest-impact gaps are not code — operator de-anonymization, platform takedown by false abuse reports, root-account takeover, and brand impersonation — several of which are ONE-WAY DOORS that are painful or impossible to retrofit after launch. This bead closes the operator/perimeter delta, weighted deliberately to free, one-time setup per the runs-itself principle. Everything stays Firebase-native (owner directive "everything will be in Firebase for now"); the two items that touch other vendors are recorded as OPTIONAL hardening with explicit triggers, never launch-blocking.

Deliver (mostly one-time human-apply steps captured as a docs/runbooks checklist + the few code/config items; this bead BLOCKS launch BB-063):
- Operator OPSEC / de-identification (pre-launch, one-way door): do NOT register the primary domain on .us (the registry forcibly publishes registrant home address — privacy prohibited, verified); use .org/.com with free registrar WHOIS privacy; register under an org / registered-agent address, not a home address; set a pseudonymous git author name + noreply email BEFORE the repo goes public (history rewrite later is disruptive); replace the personal security contact with a role address (security@domain) and a /.well-known/security.txt.
- Root-account hardening (free, blocks the worst case): hardware security keys / passkeys (WebAuthn) on GitHub AND the Google/Firebase console; remove SMS 2FA (SIM-swap vector); store recovery codes offline; enable org 2FA-required; separate the billing-owner identity from the day-to-day deploy identity so one compromise can't also max the bill. Extends BB-027 (which is app-layer admin MFA, not root-account security). Add to the BB-079 human-apply checklist.
- Platform-takedown resilience (the standard tactic is non-technical: mass false abuse reports to Google/registrar/CDN to trigger AUTOMATED suspension): keep registrar != host so one false report isn't fatal; pre-establish a paid Google Cloud support tier (even Standard) so an appeal has a human channel; write a platform-enforcement incident runbook (docs/runbooks/incidents/platform-takedown.md — contacts, an evidence pack proving the reports are false, failover steps). The Firebase-native availability backstop stays primary: App Check stops scripted abuse only (real-browser floods bypass it), so the per-project budget kill-switch + degraded snapshot mode (BB-033/035) is the true DDoS/cost backstop, and Firestore PITR/backup (BB-020) the data backstop.
  - OPTIONAL hardening, recorded with triggers (NOT required to ship, honoring "everything in Firebase for now"): front the site with Cloudflare free (DNS at a separate blast radius from Google + free edge WAF/Bot-Fight + one rate-limit rule + origin-IP hiding) and add a warm off-Google backup (periodic export to non-Google storage). Triggers to actually do it: a takedown/DDoS event occurs, OR immediately before a high-profile launch/press moment. Re-evaluate the BB-023 self-managed LB + Cloud Armor spend against Cloudflare-fronting at that point (Cloud Armor Standard has no free tier and needs a paid LB floor — verified; not worth it for a solo budget while nothing is live).
- Brand-impersonation early-warning (squatters move at announcement — be first): free crt.sh / CertSpotter certificate-transparency monitors on a curated typo/lookalike domain set, LIVE BEFORE announcement; defensive registration of the highest-risk domain variants + reserve the brand's social handles at launch; an official-properties page + rel=canonical + schema.org sameAs to verified accounts so the real site is provable; a lookalike / DMCA-clone takedown playbook.
- Legal shielding (low urgency, high impact, cheap): form an LLC to hold the project (asset shield + becomes the WHOIS registrant, feeding OPSEC above); know the applicable state anti-SLAPP statute; register a DMCA agent with the U.S. Copyright Office ($6 / 3yr) with a takedown/counter-notice runbook wired to BB-077 (BB-083's Wayback-pointer + minimal-snippet posture is the fair-use basis for counter-notices).
- Sybil / source-independence integrity (fast-follow after launch; reuses data the adapters already fetch): add NEGATIVE corroboration signals so a fake network of blogs can't manufacture apparent independent corroboration — shared RDAP registrant / ASN / nameserver, co-registration timing, domain first-seen recency, near-simultaneous publication; require >=1 top-tier source (archive/academic/newspaper-of-record) before any claim reaches published confidence (low-trust corroboration alone never publishes); add Sybil fixtures to the BB-047 gold corpus and the BB-060 adversarial exercise. Wire into BB-043 authority scoring.
