- Perimeter hygiene (free, set-and-forget or folded into the BB-084 scheduler): AI-scraper directives (robots.txt + ai.txt disallow; Cloudflare "block AI bots" if fronted); map-tile/static-asset scraping caps (BB-070 tiles are not App-Check-eligible) via long-TTL immutable cache + Referer/Origin checks; an external uptime/defacement canary (UptimeRobot free, or a GitHub-Actions cron that fetches + hashes a canary page and alerts on change/downtime); DMARC p=reject + SPF + DKIM on the domain to stop "official Black Book" email spoofing; a pseudonymous reviewer model so any future volunteer's identity never leaks via public history/audit (BB-018 keeps reviewer identity internal only).

Design: the five highest-leverage, hardest-to-retrofit items to do BEFORE any public launch are (1) operator de-identification (domain + git identity + LLC-as-registrant + role security contact), (2) root-account 2FA hardening, (3) platform-takedown resilience (runbook + support tier now; Cloudflare/off-Google backup as triggered options), (4) impersonation early-warning + official-properties, (5) launch with community submissions GATED and the graylist never obligated to be drained (a solo project cannot staff BB-076's consensus-of-N or a flooded queue on day one — make it a BB-063 launch-gate condition and a BB-076 acceptance note). Verified specifics: .us WHOIS prohibits privacy; Cloud Armor Standard has no free tier; App Check bypassable by real-browser abuse; Cloudflare free covers the solo-budget edge need; crt.sh CT monitoring is free.

Notes: This bead is framed INTERNALLY only — none of it becomes defensive public copy. The public-facing half of protection is BB-088 (rigor/trust/pre-bunking). Weighted to free + one-time per the runs-itself principle; the only recurring pieces (CT monitors, uptime canary, link-health, DMARC reports) are set-and-forget or fold into BB-084. Owner action items that only a human can do (domain, LLC, 2FA enrollment, DMCA registration, support tier) are collected into the BB-079 / operator runbook so a periodic session can execute them in one sitting. Blocks BB-063 (launch).
design: Top 5 pre-launch hardest-to-retrofit: operator de-identification, root 2FA, platform-takedown resilience, impersonation early-warning, launch with submissions GATED + graylist never obligated to drain (a solo op cannot staff BB-076 consensus-of-N or a flooded queue day one - a BB-063 gate + BB-076 acceptance note). Verified: .us WHOIS prohibits privacy; Cloud Armor Standard no free tier; App Check bypassable by real-browser abuse; Cloudflare free covers solo edge; crt.sh free. Everything Firebase-native per owner directive; the two cross-vendor items are triggered options only.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Operator OPSEC applied pre-launch (non-.us domain w/ registrar WHOIS privacy, org/registered-agent registrant address not home, pseudonymous git author+noreply set before repo goes public, role security contact + security.txt). 2. Root-account hardening (WebAuthn hardware keys/passkeys on GitHub AND Google/Firebase console, SMS 2FA removed, offline recovery codes, org 2FA-required, billing-owner separated from deploy identity) - added to BB-079 checklist. 3. Platform-takedown resilience (registrar != host, paid Google support tier for a human appeal channel, docs/runbooks/incidents/platform-takedown.md); Firebase-native availability backstop primary (budget kill-switch + degraded snapshot BB-033/035, PITR BB-020); Cloudflare-fronting + off-Google backup recorded as OPTIONAL with explicit triggers, not required to ship. 4. Brand-impersonation early-warning live before announcement (crt.sh/CertSpotter CT monitors on a lookalike set, defensive domain/social-handle registration, official-properties page + rel=canonical + sameAs, clone/DMCA takedown playbook). 5. Legal shielding (LLC as registrant, state anti-SLAPP known, DMCA agent registered $6/3yr wired to BB-077). 6. Sybil source-independence signals added to BB-043 + fixtures to BB-047/BB-060; >=1 top-tier source required before published confidence. 7. Perimeter hygiene (robots/ai.txt, tile-scrape caps, uptime/defacement canary, DMARC p=reject+SPF+DKIM, pseudonymous reviewer model). 8. Blocks BB-063 launch; owner-only action items collected into the operator runbook.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17 (buildable half only, per the bead's own safety framing — no domains registered, no accounts touched, no 2FA changed, no payments made):

Real code/config: packages/domain/src/confidence-engine/sybil-signals.ts (+11 tests) — Sybil/source-independence signals. Shared RDAP-registrant/ASN/nameserver overlap collapses lineage roots onto one canonical id BEFORE calling the existing recalculateConfidence (reuses BB-017's syndicated-copy dedupe rather than reimplementing scoring — documented in the module's own header comment). Co-registration timing, domain first-seen recency, and near-simultaneous publication are advisory-only (recorded, never silently change the score). A hard gate requires >=1 top-tier source (primary_archival/government_record/peer_reviewed) before passesPublishThreshold — ANDed onto the existing gate, not a parallel one. 5 new Sybil fixtures added to the BB-047 gold corpus (gc-121-gc-125, source_lineage category; corpus bumped to 125 examples/v1.1.0) with matching before/after prediction entries showing a naive system fooled by manufactured corroboration vs. one that correctly withholds publication.

apps/web/src/app/robots.ts, ai.txt/route.ts, .well-known/security.txt/route.ts (RFC 9116, clearly-templated blackbook.example placeholder) — verified live via next build + next start + curl. .github/workflows/canary-uptime.yml — real, SHA-pinned uptime/defacement canary (schedule trigger intentionally commented out until a real domain exists). docs/runbooks/pre-launch-operator-protection.md, docs/runbooks/incidents/platform-takedown.md, docs/ui/official-properties.md.

Human-only checklist items (every numbered checkbox in pre-launch-operator-protection.md): domain/registrar choice + WHOIS privacy, LLC formation, pseudonymous git identity, hardware-key enrollment on GitHub/Google, removing SMS 2FA, paid GCP support tier, CT monitoring setup, defensive domain/handle registration, DMCA agent registration (/3yr), DMARC/SPF/DKIM DNS record templates (values only the domain owner can create).

Shared-file handling: confidence-engine/engine.ts was concurrently edited by 3 other agents this session (BB-073/076/083) — this agent never touched it, only imported from it read-only (sybil-signals.ts is a sibling file, not an edit to engine.ts itself). confidence-engine/index.ts and domain/package.json saw concurrent additive edits from multiple agents alongside this one's — all coexist correctly, confirmed by the parent session's full-suite re-run after all 5 agents landed.

Necessary edits outside the literal file-ownership list (disclosed by the delivering agent): a one-line example-count bump each in packages/testing/src/gold-corpus/gold-corpus.test.ts and retrieval-eval.test.ts (both had hard-coded counts that would otherwise fail once the 5 Sybil fixtures were added) — minimal, required for the additive fixtures to pass, not a scope violation in substance.

Parent-session fix required: apps/web's existing runtime-hardening static guard (FORBIDDEN_PUBLIC_RENDER_IMPORTS, a public-render-path LLM/DB-import detector) was a bare substring match on /anthropic/ and /openai/ with no import-syntax requirement, so robots.ts's legitimate 'anthropic-ai' crawler-blocklist entry tripped it as a false positive (same bug class as repo-a15e7132, now generalized). Fixed by requiring actual import/require syntax context around all the bare-word patterns in apps/web/src/lib/runtime-hardening/constants.ts, and updated its own test fixture to still exercise real detection without re-triggering the repo-wide boundary scanner (mirrors the e9z fix pattern exactly). Verified: web test suite 56/56, a real next build (21 routes incl. /robots.txt, /ai.txt, /.well-known/security.txt), pnpm validate clean.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after all 5 concurrent agents in this wave landed): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 392/392 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/testing test 51/51 pass (3 pre-existing skips), typecheck clean; @black-book/web typecheck clean + real build clean. pnpm validate passes clean end to end.
close_reason: Sybil/source-independence signals (lineage-collapse for strong shared-infrastructure evidence, advisory-only timing signals, a hard top-tier-source publication gate) with 5 new gold-corpus fixtures; robots.txt/ai.txt/security.txt live and verified; a real SHA-pinned uptime canary workflow; and three considered runbooks/docs. Every real-world account/domain/payment action is an explicit human-only checklist item, never attempted. Parent session fixed a real false-positive in the public-render-path import guard that robots.ts's legitimate AI-crawler blocklist tripped.

title: BB-087: Legal landscape (snapshot corpus, change monitoring, plain-language access)
description:
Expansion bead BB-087 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (legal landscape: snapshot corpus + change monitoring + plain-language access).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("The law will play a big part in this and I want to get the law included here... get a snapshot of current state, and have some way of scraping for changes around those things based on the publicly available sources. We want to make this all accessible to people who don't know how to get it")
Plan: plan.md
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-086, BB-030, BB-037, BB-084

Why: the owner wants U.S. civil-rights law made first-class and, crucially, accessible to people who don't know how to find or read it. Two halves: a curated SNAPSHOT of the laws/cases that matter to the mission, and ongoing MONITORING that detects changes from free public sources into a human review queue. Every source below is free and public-domain or open; everything runs Firebase-native (external APIs/bulk files fetched by workers into Firestore + Storage — no data leaves Firebase). The law is a `claimType` in the BB-086 fact registry, not a separate store: statutes and cases become fact records with legal-citation CSL-JSON, sharing the same permalink/citation/revision machinery.

Deliver — SNAPSHOT (one-time curated seed, all free):
- Federal statutes (curated ~50: Civil Rights Act 1964, Voting Rights Act 1965, Fair Housing Act 1968, 42 USC 1981/1983, Reconstruction Amendments, etc.): canonical text from OLRC USLM XML release points (uscode.house.gov, maintained within days — current release point PL 119-102) + as-enacted Statutes at Large / Public Laws from GovInfo bulk; status metadata from Congress.gov API v3 (free api.data.gov key, 5,000 req/hr).
- Federal regulations (24/28/29/34 CFR civil-rights parts): point-in-time text from the eCFR Versioner API (no key) + GovInfo CFR annual editions (1996+); rulemaking context from the Federal Register API (no key).
- Landmark cases (SCOTUS + federal appellate, Dred Scott -> Brown -> Shelby County -> SFFA): opinion text from CourtListener quarterly BULK CSVs (free, Public Domain Mark — the workhorse that sidesteps the thin new API tier) + supremecourt.gov slip PDFs archived; structured metadata from SCDB (scdb.la.psu.edu, CC BY-NC 3.0, attribute); plain-English summaries link to Oyez (api.oyez.org, link + short attributed quotes only). Validate every case cite via CourtListener Citation Lookup API before publish.
- State law in four high-relevance areas (education/curriculum-restriction, voting access, housing/fair-housing, policing accountability): bill text + official state-code links from LegiScan (free tier 30,000 queries/mo; seed via weekly dataset ZIPs at ~1 query/session) with Open States/Plural bulk (monthly, public-domain dedication) as a redundant path. Topical tagging seeded from CRT Forward via IPUMS CDOH (curriculum, 2020-2024) + PEN America Index (current curriculum, pointer only) + Brennan Center roundups & Voting Rights Lab tracker (voting, secondary/link) + NCSL databases (secondary/link). No free national state-CODE API exists: link the official state-code site (LegiScan `state_link`), archive the capture, never scrape Justia.
- Enforcement-reality layer (public-domain XLSX, mirrored to Firebase Storage at ingest): EEOC charge/litigation stats by race, HUD FHEO filed Title VIII cases, DOJ Civil Rights Division actions. WARNING: 2025-26 federal civil-rights web content is being actively removed (DOJ consent-decree dismissals/retractions documented) — archive.org Save Page Now on every capture and mirror every dataset into Storage; expect link rot (feeds BB-083).
- Every stored legal object carries: canonical citation, source URL, retrieval date, archived capture pointer, and a LICENSE TAG (public-domain vs CC BY-NC vs link-only) — the NC-licensed sources (SCDB, Wex, Oyez) degrade to link-only if the project ever monetizes.

Deliver — MONITORING (scheduled via BB-084, framed as BB-037 discovery adapters; automation PROPOSES, humans DISPOSE):
- Federal: Congress.gov `/law` + GovInfo PLAW RSS (new public laws, daily); Federal Register API filtered to civil-rights agencies + public-inspection endpoint (reg changes, daily); eCFR Versioner title-version diff on watched parts (weekly); Regulations.gov API on watched dockets (weekly).
- Cases: CourtListener free SEARCH ALERTS + CITATION ALERTS (daily email digests, OUTSIDE the API quota) for new opinions citing watched cases; free docket alerts (5, +10 with the RECAP extension) on key active dockets.
- State: LegiScan `getMasterListRaw` `change_hash` diff per watched session -> `getBill` on changes (daily for ~10 priority states, weekly others; well under 30k/mo); Open States `updated_since` topic queries as a redundant path (within free 500/day).
- Curated cross-checks: page-hash change detection on Brennan/VRL/PEN/NCSL pages -> human task (link, never republish).
- Change events land in a Firestore `review_queue` with the shape: {source, event_type (bill_status_change|became_law|new_opinion|reg_amended|cfr_version|tracker_update), jurisdiction, topic[], external_id, title, summary_snippet, detected_at, evidence:{source_url, official_url, archived_capture_url, change_hash_prev/new, diff_hint}, proposed_action, affected_entries[], confidence, status:pending_review}. Dedupe key (source, external_id, change_hash_new); every event carries an archived capture before it is reviewable.

Deliver — PLAIN-LANGUAGE ACCESS (the owner's "accessible to people who don't know how to get it"):
- Authoring pattern per law/case, reusing BB-086 fact records + BB-088 trust voice: (1) What the law says — one-sentence holding/provision + exact citation + status badge (in force / amended / struck down / enjoined); (2) What it means — 2-4 short active-voice paragraphs at ~6th-8th grade reading level, one idea per sentence, every term of art linked to Wex rather than re-defined; (3) Why it matters for Black Americans — historical context tied to map entries; (4) Your rights today / current status — bullets linking official complaint channels (EEOC/HUD/DOJ), never paraphrasing ACLU; (5) Primary sources — canonical text link + archived capture + secondary reading. Standing "not legal advice" disclaimer; every explainer date-stamped ("reviewed YYYY-MM-DD").
- Licensing discipline for the layer: AUTHOR yourself + cite primary (default); PUBLIC-DOMAIN quote/adapt freely with credit (Constitution Annotated, CRS via EveryCRSReport bulk, plainlanguage.gov / digital.gov Federal Plain Language Guidelines, all agency explainers); LINK + short attributed quotes only (Wex CC BY-NC-SA, Oyez, SCDB); DO NOT republish (ACLU Know Your Rights — written permission required; link only).

Design: CourtListener bulk CSVs (free/PD) for corpus + free alert emails for monitoring is the entire budget answer — the paid API tier (125 req/day free since 2026-05-07) is avoided; cheapest paid fallback is a free .edu membership if any academic affiliation exists, else Tier 1 $10/mo (flag as an owner decision, not required to ship). Bot-shielded .gov pages (Federal Register/eCFR/Justia/PEN 403 non-browser UAs): use the APIs not HTML, set an honest User-Agent with backoff, run from Cloud Functions; docs live on GitHub (usgpo, LibraryOfCongress) when sites block. All Firebase-native.

Notes: This directly serves the owner's "get the law included... snapshot of current state... scraping for changes... accessible to people who don't know how to get it." Reuses BB-086 (fact records), BB-030 (URL-safety fetch), BB-037 (adapter/registry discipline), BB-083 (link-rot/repair), BB-084 (scheduler) — no parallel systems. Policing accountability is an acknowledged gap (no free national tracker since NCSL's DB went stale): covered by LegiScan saved searches ("qualified immunity", "duty to intervene", "police discipline") + human triage, not a turnkey feed. Manual-verify-before-relying items flagged in research: Oyez footer license, PEN index sheet license, NCSL/LegiScan ToS, Open States rate table, Justia ToS.

Jurisdiction and status alignment: every law fact record resolves jurisdictionId to the BB-091 jurisdictions collection; effect-area rendering derives geometry from jurisdiction reference data, never a point; the status badge vocabulary (in force / amended / struck down / enjoined) is the BB-090 law-kind status lifecycle — one definition, imported.
design: CourtListener bulk CSVs (free/PD) for corpus + free alert emails for monitoring is the whole budget answer (the paid API tier is avoided; free .edu membership or $10/mo Tier 1 is the flagged fallback, not required). Bot-shielded .gov pages: use APIs not HTML, honest UA + backoff from Cloud Functions. Licensing verdicts recorded per source: public-domain (quote freely), CC BY-NC (link+attribute, degrade if monetized), ACLU KYR (link only, permission required). Reuses BB-086 fact records, BB-030 fetch safety, BB-037 adapter discipline, BB-083 link-rot, BB-084 scheduler - no parallel systems.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Curated federal snapshot ingested from free public-domain sources: statutes (OLRC USLM XML release points + GovInfo Statutes at Large/PLAW + Congress.gov API), regs (eCFR Versioner + GovInfo CFR + Federal Register API), landmark cases (CourtListener bulk CSVs + supremecourt.gov PDFs + SCDB metadata + Oyez link/quote), every case cite validated via CourtListener Citation Lookup. 2. State law across curriculum/voting/housing/policing from LegiScan free tier + Open States bulk, official state-code linked+archived (no scraping Justia), topically seeded from CRT Forward via IPUMS + PEN + Brennan + VRL + NCSL. 3. Enforcement layer (EEOC/HUD-FHEO/DOJ) mirrored to Firebase Storage at ingest with Wayback capture (federal content is being removed). 4. Every legal object carries citation + source URL + retrieval date + archived capture + license tag (NC-licensed sources degrade to link-only). 5. Scheduled monitoring (BB-084) across Congress.gov/GovInfo RSS, Federal Register API, eCFR version diff, CourtListener free search+citation+docket alerts, LegiScan change_hash diff, Open States updated_since, and page-hash tracker cross-checks, landing dedup'd change events in a review_queue (automation proposes, humans dispose) each with an archived capture before review. 6. Plain-language explainer per law/case (What it says / What it means / Why it matters for Black Americans / Your rights today / Primary sources) at ~6-8th grade level, term-of-art links to Wex, official complaint-channel links, not-legal-advice disclaimer, date-stamped. 7. All Firebase-native; no data leaves Firebase. 8. Every statute/case fact record resolves jurisdictionId to a BB-091 jurisdictions doc (federal|state|county|municipal); effect-area/map rendering derives geometry from jurisdiction reference data, never a lat/lng point; law status badges use the BB-090 law-kind status vocabulary (single shared definition).
notes: Serves owner's get-the-law-in + snapshot + scrape-for-changes + accessible-to-people-who-dont-know-how-to-get-it. Policing accountability is an acknowledged gap (no free national tracker): LegiScan saved searches + human triage. Manual-verify-before-relying: Oyez/PEN/NCSL/LegiScan/OpenStates/Justia terms (flagged in research). Sized XL - may split into corpus-seed and monitoring sub-beads at execution.
close_reason: Shipped BB-087 legal landscape framework in-repo: domain legal module (snapshots, review_queue, explainers, monitoring), fixture-only adapters (Congress.gov/eCFR/CourtListener/LegiScan), /legal browse+detail UI with plain-language sections and not-legal-advice disclaimer, 12-entry seed catalog with FactRecord links (BB-F-000010–021), legal-change-monitoring scheduled job. 26 targeted tests pass. Parent wiring pending: packages/domain/src/index.ts export legal module, packages/domain/src/adapters/index.ts export legal adapters, packages/config scheduled-jobs roster.ts + index.ts register legal-change-monitoring job. Live vendor keys (api.data.gov Congress.gov, LegiScan API) documented for human follow-up — fixtures-only in tests.

title: BB-086: Canonical fact registry (the fact framework)
description:
Expansion bead BB-086 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (canonical fact registry: the fact framework).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("a solid fact framework. This framework will solve two purposes. It will be the fact repository, serving as a searchable section of the application, but it will also be referenced by some of the other information surfaces in the site, eg fact page referenced alongside specific info with both surfaces linking to same reference info")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-016, BB-019, BB-049, BB-053

Why: the owner wants a fact framework serving two purposes at once — a searchable fact repository AND a shared reference layer that other surfaces (map popups, place/event/entity pages) cite, with both surfaces linking to the same canonical record and the same citations. The domain already models claims/evidence/confidence (BB-014) and a dispute/revision interface exists (BB-053), but there is no first-class, independently addressable, permalinked "fact" entity that can be browsed as a library and embedded everywhere from one source of truth. Under hostile scrutiny the decisive asset is exactly this: a canonical, versioned, citable fact with a stable per-revision permalink, so an attacker's out-of-context screenshot is answered by linking the live cited record and its history. Reuses the existing claims/evidence model rather than building a parallel system.

Deliver:
- FactRecord entity + schema in @black-book/domain, modeled on the Wikidata statement model (statement + qualifiers + structured references + rank): canonical `statement` (one declarative sentence, the citable text) + `shortStatement` (<=75 chars for markup/popups/embeds); `claimType` enum (event | person-fact | law | place-designation | quantity | quote-attribution) driving required geo/time fields and emitted schema.org type; `subjects[]` typed edges to Place/Person/Event/Law entities; `geo` + `geoPrecision` (exact-site | block | locality | county | state); `when` + `datePrecision` (day | month | year | decade | circa) with optional range; `qualifiers[]` small typed key/values (as-reported-by, estimated, disputed-by-contemporaries, name-variant); `counterClaims[]` (circulating misreadings + one-line refutation, feeds BB-088 pre-bunking and the myths surface); `relatedFacts[]` typed edges (supports | contradicts | contextualizes | supersedes | partOf | sameEventAs); `provenance` (researchedBy, reviewedBy, reviewedAt, method).
- Two independent axes, never conflated: `status` workflow rank (draft | under_review | published | corrected | superseded | deprecated — deprecated/superseded records stay resolvable with a banner + reason, never 404) and `confidence` evidence grade (established | corroborated | single-source | contested, with definitions published on the methodology page and nuance carried in a `confidenceNote`, per the industry move to small scales + rich prose).
- `citations[]` stored as CSL-JSON plus a Black Book extension block per citation: sourceClass (primary | secondary | tertiary), role (supports | contradicts | contextualizes), excerpt (the supporting passage), pageLocator, url, archivedUrl + archivedAt, accessedAt, documentId (pointer into the primary-document store), sourceNote. Every web citation carries an archived-capture pointer (BB-016) + retrieval date; "unsourced" is not a publishable state — completeness enforced fail-closed at BB-019 projection build (shared gate with BB-083).
- Stable identity + permalinks: immutable, never-reused `BB-F-######` id; `/facts/{id}/{slug}` (cosmetic slug 301s on change); per-revision `/facts/{id}/rev/{n}` (the Wikipedia oldid pattern — the single most important hostile-quoting defense).
- Shared-reference model (the owner's core requirement): the fact is the atom; the national map (BB-051/070), place/event/entity pages (BB-052), and the evidence interface (BB-053) all render compact views that reference the SAME record and citation set — one canonical URL in every embed, edited in one place. The fact library is a searchable section over published facts via the BB-049 search lane.
- Machine-readable output: per-fact `Article` JSON-LD (about + citation + dateModified + version + correction); content-negotiated `/facts/{id}.json` (CSL-JSON + extension block) so the registry is consumable as data; canonical facts are NEVER wrapped in ClaimReview (that misdescribes an assertion as a review and violates Google eligibility) — ClaimReview lives only on the BB-088 myths surface.
- Append-only `revisions[]`: rev, timestamp, agent, changeType (correction | clarification | update | style — PolitiFact taxonomy), mandatory edit `summary`, diff; corrections fan out to the global errata log (BB-088). PROV-O naming (Entity/Activity/Agent, wasRevisionOf) on the revision/audit tables so a future RDF export is mechanical.
- Citation rendering via citation.js (@citation-js/core, MIT) from stored CSL-JSON; `documentId` abstracts document hosting so the backend is swappable (DocumentCloud pending verification / self-host + SHA-256 fallback, decided with BB-016).

Design: steal Wikidata's discipline — sources are first-class, retrieval dates mandatory for web sources, superseded facts kept-not-deleted with a reason. All standards are open and free (schema.org CC BY-SA, CSL-JSON schema MIT, citation.js MIT, PROV-O a stable W3C Rec). This is the backing model for BB-053's UI, not a competing one.

Notes: Directly answers the owner's "fact page referenced alongside specific info with both surfaces linking to same reference info." The law (BB-087) is a `claimType`, not a separate store — statutes/cases become fact records with legal-citation CSL-JSON. Frame every field choice around one goal the copy never states aloud: make each fact independently verifiable by a hostile reader.

Ontology alignment (2026-07-17 second message): datePrecision and geoPrecision vocabularies are imported from the shared domain modules established by BB-090/BB-091 — single definition, no local redefinition; subjects[] edges resolve to CanonicalEntity ids and BB-090 kinds; place-designation fact records are the claim backing for time-scoped entity statusHistory entries (BB-090).

Graph cross-reference (2026-07-17 stress test): subjects[] typed edges are a graph-view input for BB-092 — implementers should treat subjects[] as feeding the published browse graph (directly or via publish-time mirroring into EntityRelationship), not only fact-page display; this closes a gap where an entity linked to a fact only through subjects[] would otherwise be invisible to the history graph.
design: Wikidata statement+qualifier+reference+rank model (sources first-class, retrieval dates mandatory, superseded kept-not-deleted); PROV-O naming on revision/audit tables for a mechanical future RDF export; CSL-JSON + citation.js (MIT); per-revision permalinks = Wikipedia oldid pattern, the single most important hostile-quoting defense. All standards open/free (schema.org CC BY-SA, CSL-JSON MIT, PROV-O W3C Rec). Backing model for BB-053's UI, not a competing one.
acceptance_criteria: 1. FactRecord entity + schema in @black-book/domain modeled on Wikidata statements (statement + shortStatement + qualifiers + typed subjects/relatedFacts + counterClaims + provenance), with two independent axes status and confidence, deprecated/superseded records stay resolvable never 404. 2. Citations stored as CSL-JSON + extension block (sourceClass, role, excerpt, url, archivedUrl+archivedAt, accessedAt, documentId); no published claim lacks a citation with an archived-capture pointer + retrieval date, enforced fail-closed at BB-019 projection build. 3. Immutable BB-F-###### ids; /facts/{id}/{slug} plus per-revision /facts/{id}/rev/{n} permalinks resolve. 4. The same fact record is referenced by the map (BB-051/070), entity/place/event pages (BB-052), and the evidence UI (BB-053) from one canonical URL and one citation set. 5. Searchable fact library over published facts via the BB-049 lane. 6. Per-fact Article JSON-LD + content-negotiated /facts/{id}.json emitted; canonical facts are never wrapped in ClaimReview. 7. Append-only revisions with mandatory edit summaries; citations render via citation.js from stored CSL-JSON. 8. datePrecision and geoPrecision vocabularies are imported from the shared @black-book/domain modules (BB-090/BB-091), not redefined locally; every subjects[] edge resolves to a CanonicalEntity id and kind; place-designation fact records can back BB-090 statusHistory entries. 9. subjects[] edges are confirmed as a BB-092 graph-view input (directly consumed or mirrored into EntityRelationship at publish time), documented so fact-only entity linkages are not silently absent from the published graph.
notes: Answers owner's solid-fact-framework ask: searchable repository + shared reference layer where a fact page and other surfaces link to the same reference record. Law (BB-087) is a claimType, not a separate store. Reuses BB-014 claims/evidence model per runs-itself principle. Every field choice serves one unstated goal: make each fact independently verifiable by a hostile reader.
close_reason: BB-086 AC met: FactRecord domain module (record/citation/revision/publish-gate/ids/search-index/jsonld/embed/subjects) with 83 tests; public web surfaces at /facts, /facts/{id}/{slug}, /facts/{id}/rev/{n}, /facts/{id}.json; seed catalog + components; fail-closed citations; Article JSON-LD never ClaimReview; subjects[] documented as BB-092 graph input.

title: BB-079: Production cloud apply (consolidated live provisioning)
description:
Expansion bead BB-079 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (production cloud apply — consolidated).

Source: bead audit 2026-07-17 (unapplied steps recovered from close comments of BB-005/009/010/011/020/021/023/024/025/027/034/035)
Plan: plan.md
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-078

Why: roughly a dozen closed beads are repo-complete but cloud-incomplete — their "human cloud steps remaining" live only in close comments and READMEs, with no tracking bead. Nothing can actually launch until these are applied, and they must be applied against the BB-078 topology so nothing is provisioned twice in the wrong project.

Deliver (each item gets a checklist entry with a verification command and evidence recorded on this bead):
- GitHub: create the remote repository, push, apply the declarative rulesets/CODEOWNERS/allowed-actions from infra/github/ (BB-009); fix gh auth.
- WIF/OIDC: apply the Terraform under infra/ with real numeric GitHub IDs; per-environment deploy SAs with CEL conditions per BB-078 (BB-010).
- Firebase projects: create blackbook-staging and blackbook-internal per BB-078; Blaze upgrade(s); enable Firestore databases (including internal's named raw-ingest/curated DBs); deploy rules and indexes from infra/firebase/ (BB-011/BB-013).
- Buckets and service accounts: the 11 named SAs and 4 buckets (public-media, private-evidence, exports, quarantine) with PAP/UBLA and per-secret IAM (BB-005).
- App Check: register reCAPTCHA Enterprise provider, monitor-mode rollout, then enforce (BB-024).
- Cloud Armor + ingress: apply the designed policies, ALB/NEG/CDN wiring, rate_based_ban and emergency rules (BB-023).
- Rate limits: provision Memorystore/Redis backing and wire the token-bucket evaluators (BB-025).
- Backups: Firestore PITR + scheduled exports + bucket retention/IAM-deny-delete; schedule the quarterly restore drill (BB-020/BB-061).
- Admin: Cloud Run + IAP for the console, admin Google group, MFA policy verification (BB-027 per BB-078 correction).
- Telemetry: create the alert policies/dashboards from the BB-034 stubs; verify security events flow.

Acceptance is binary per line item: applied + verified + evidenced, or explicitly deferred with reason on this bead. Closing this bead unblocks BB-062 (release pipeline) for real.
design: Consolidates the unapplied human/cloud steps recovered from close comments of BB-005/009/010/011/020/021/023/024/025/027/034/035 - previously tracked nowhere. Must be applied against the BB-078 topology so nothing lands in the wrong project.
acceptance_criteria: Binary per roster item - applied + verification command output + evidence recorded on this bead, or explicitly deferred with reason: 1. GitHub remote + rulesets/CODEOWNERS/allowed-actions applied; gh auth working. 2. WIF/OIDC applied with real numeric IDs and per-env CEL conditions. 3. Projects created per BB-078; Blaze; Firestore DBs enabled (incl. internal named DBs); rules + indexes deployed. 4. 11 SAs + 4 buckets (PAP/UBLA) + per-secret IAM. 5. App Check: reCAPTCHA Enterprise registered, monitor then enforce. 6. Cloud Armor policies + ingress applied. 7. Memorystore/Redis rate-limit backing wired. 8. PITR + scheduled exports + retention/IAM-deny-delete + quarterly drill scheduled. 9. Admin console on Cloud Run behind IAP with admin group + MFA verified. 10. Telemetry alert policies/dashboards live and receiving events. Closing this bead unblocks BB-062.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: docs/runbooks/production-cloud-apply-checklist.md — a single consolidated document gathering every 'human cloud step remaining' note scattered across BB-005/009/010/011/020/021/023/024/025/027/034/035/061's close comments and READMEs, reorganized against the ADR-012 three-project topology. 10 numbered sections matching the bead's exact roster (GitHub remote+rulesets, WIF/OIDC, Firebase projects+Firestore+rules/indexes, buckets+SAs, App Check, Cloud Armor, rate-limit backing store, backups+drill, admin Cloud Run+IAP, telemetry). Each section: why (traced to the source bead's actual close note), exact commands with real file-path references (verified to exist, not guessed), a verification command/expected output, and a status line — every item is DEFERRED (requires live cloud/account action outside any agent session's authority), per the bead's own binary acceptance model ('applied+verified+evidenced, or explicitly deferred with reason').

Self-audited: zero live gcloud/firebase/terraform-apply/gh-repo-create commands were run; only read-only inspection (bd show, Read, grep/find, git status) plus writing the one document.

Found and flagged (not fixed here, per file ownership) 4 genuine inconsistencies in BB-078's own artifacts that would cause real provisioning errors if section 4/8 were applied as literally written today (SA topology conflict between locals.tf and deploy-roles.md, undefined private-evidence bucket location, missing rules/indexes deploy target for named Firestore databases, hardcoded-off PITR on named databases with no variable gate) plus 3 already-known gaps repeated for completeness (stale IAP README, no Memorystore artifact, unimplemented submissions-puller). Filed repo-d4e38d6a to track fixing these 4 new findings before BB-079's sections 3/4/8 are ever applied for real.
close_reason: Consolidated cloud-apply checklist delivered as a single 10-section runbook, every item correctly deferred per the bead's binary acceptance model. Found and filed (black-book-2ve) 4 real infra-artifact inconsistencies that need reconciling before live apply.

title: BB-078: Production environment re-split (multi-project isolation)
description:
Expansion bead BB-078 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (production environment re-split).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("this project is production... isolating for security purposes... I'd like to stay in Firebase") + isolation research (Firebase/GCP primary docs)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-005, BB-021

Why: D-013 collapsed the planned four-project topology into the single project black-book-efaaf, where "staging" is a configuration namespace and ADR-009 itself admits "weaker blast-radius isolation than a project split." Firebase's official guidance is one project per environment, and the re-split trigger the docs promised ("before production launch") is now. Everything below stays inside Firebase/GCP.

Deliver:
- ADR-012 superseding D-013: three-project topology —
  - blackbook-prod: public serving (App Hosting backend, Firestore projections + create-only submissions collection, Auth, App Check enforced, prod secrets only);
  - blackbook-staging: mirror of prod shape, synthetic data only, minInstances 0;
  - blackbook-internal: research pipeline + admin. Named Firestore databases raw-ingest and curated with per-database IAM conditions (GA feature; hotspot isolation + per-DB rules + per-DB billing visibility). Admin console runs as a plain Cloud Run service behind Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP-on-Cloud-Run needs no load balancer; App Hosting backends cannot sit behind IAP — this corrects BB-021's assumption). Local dev stays on emulators (demo-black-book).
- One-way promotion enforced by asymmetric IAM: the internal promotion service account is the ONLY principal with datastore write into blackbook-prod; no prod principal has any IAM in internal. Submissions flow the reverse direction via an internal puller reading prod's create-only collection. Optional hardening: VPC Service Controls perimeter around blackbook-internal with a single egress rule for the promotion SA.
- CI/CD identity split: one WIF pool, per-project deploy SAs, CEL attribute conditions pinning repository AND ref (prod deploys only from the release branch/protected environment); org policy iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation; cross-environment deploys fail at IAM, not convention.
- Secret Manager per project; budgets + alerts per project (billing kill-switch acceptable on internal only, never on prod).
- Migration plan from black-book-efaaf (becomes blackbook-prod or is replaced — decide in the ADR while nothing live depends on it; this is the cheapest moment the project will ever have for this migration) and restatement of AC-ISO-1..5 as cross-project invariants with tests.
design: Firebase official guidance (primary docs): separate project per environment; multi-env-in-one-project explicitly not recommended for App Hosting; Auth/App Check/secrets/billing all split for free at the project boundary. Named Firestore DBs (GA) give per-DB IAM/rules/billing WITHIN internal for pipeline stages - not an environment substitute. IAP now attaches directly to Cloud Run (no LB needed); App Hosting backends cannot sit behind IAP - corrects a BB-021 assumption. Optional VPC-SC perimeter around internal with a single promotion egress rule makes one-way flow policy-enforced.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ADR-012 committed superseding the D-013 single-project decision in docs/security/environment-isolation.md. 2. Declarative/Terraform configs for the three-project topology (blackbook-prod, blackbook-staging, blackbook-internal) including internal named Firestore databases (raw-ingest, curated) with per-database IAM conditions. 3. One-way promotion IAM asymmetry encoded: promotion SA is the only principal with write into prod; zero prod IAM into internal; submissions pulled by internal from the prod create-only collection. 4. WIF per environment with CEL conditions pinning repository AND ref; SA key creation disabled via org policy (or documented equivalent without an org). 5. AC-ISO-1..5 restated as cross-project invariants with tests. 6. Migration runbook from black-book-efaaf, including the admin-console correction (Cloud Run + IAP, not App Hosting).
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: ADR-012 (docs/adr/ADR-012-production-environment-resplit.md) supersedes D-013 with the three-project topology — blackbook-prod (retains black-book-efaaf's project ID since GCP project IDs are immutable and nothing live depends on it yet), blackbook-staging (new), blackbook-internal (new, named Firestore DBs raw-ingest/curated with per-DB IAM conditions). D-013 marked superseded in .cx/decisions/, not deleted.

One-way promotion IAM asymmetry encoded in infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf: 4 cross-project grants (promotion->prod Firestore, promotion->prod buckets, security->prod quarantine, submissions-puller->prod submissions), zero prod->internal grants. New module infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/ (projects, Firestore DBs, SAs, cross-project IAM, org-policy backstops, per-project budgets — prod is notify-only by construction, no prod kill-switch resource exists anywhere in the module). infra/gcp/wif/terraform/ extended (not duplicated) with per-project deploy SAs (github-deploy-internal added, github-deploy-staging re-scoped). Every resource gated behind a boolean defaulting false — nothing applied.

infra/gcp/isolation-matrix.json extended: productionResplitTarget, crossProjectGrants, promotion/submissions-puller identities, AC-ISO-1..5 restated as cross-project invariants — validated against its JSON Schema and covered by a new test (infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/tests/isolation-invariants.test.mjs, 6/6 passing).

Migration runbook: docs/runbooks/production-environment-resplit-migration.md — 19-step human checklist with exact gcloud/terraform commands, verification table, rollback-cost-by-phase.

Validation (all re-run and confirmed by the parent session, not just the subagent): terraform validate clean for both infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project and infra/gcp/wif/terraform; isolation-matrix.json passes JSON Schema validation; isolation-invariants.test.mjs 6/6 pass; grep swept for secrets/keys — clean.

Two genuine follow-on gaps flagged by the delivering agent (NOT fixed in this bead — outside its file ownership): (1) infra/gcp/iap/README.md (BB-027 territory) still assumes the older external-LB + serverless-NEG IAP pattern and needs a rewrite for direct-attach Cloud Run IAP before the admin migration step is executed for real. (2) The runbook/matrix name and grant a 'submissions-puller' identity/surface, but its actual Firestore-pull logic isn't implemented anywhere (workers/ ownership) — the grant is declared but unconsumed until a follow-up bead builds the puller worker.
close_reason: Three-project topology declaratively designed (ADR-012, Terraform validated-not-applied, isolation matrix extended + tested, migration runbook). No live cloud apply, per project convention. Two follow-on gaps flagged (IAP rewrite, submissions-puller worker).

title: BB-070: Map data platform and tiles
description:
Expansion bead BB-070 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (map data platform).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("the map is a huge element of what we are doing") + ADR-008/ADR-011 + code survey
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-015, BB-019, BB-026

Why: the map is a flagship product element, but no map library exists anywhere in the repo — the current MapFrame positions pins by hardcoded x/y percentages. The domain layer already has real geo primitives (geohash encode/prefix, Point/Polygon/BBox, public precision levels) and the redaction layer explicitly protects maps. This bead builds the data platform under BB-051's map experience; U.S. scope only (50 states + D.C.).

Deliver:
- ADR-012 (map stack): MapLibre GL JS (BSD, no vendor lock-in, works with App Check'd endpoints) + tile strategy — self-hosted Protomaps PMTiles served from Firebase Hosting/CDN preferred for cost control and independence; managed MapTiler as the fallback if basemap authoring costs too much time. Custom dark, desaturated basemap style (archive-of-record register; light tourism basemaps rejected — see BB-051 design notes).
- Geo projection pipeline: on release activation, build the map source from active public projections (GeoJSON now; PMTiles/vector when point volume demands) — geohash + coarsened lat/lng only, redaction (BB-015) enforced upstream so precise living-person residences can never reach a map artifact. Rebuild is part of the release pipeline, versioned with the release like the search index.
- Everything-active population: the map source includes EVERY published entity with a geographic anchor in the active release, not a curated subset — the "history is everywhere" thesis is a data contract.
- Precomputed presence/density aggregates (state + county) so coverage renders even at national zoom (presence rendering, not just pins).
- Perf budget: national view interactive < 3s on mid-tier mobile; point/tile budget documented; map query endpoints (if any beyond static sources) behind BB-026 guardrails + App Check.
- Degraded mode: map serves from the release snapshot like the rest of the public surface (ADR-004).
design: MapLibre GL JS: BSD license, no vendor lock, self-host friendly - fits stay-in-Firebase economics (tiles on Firebase Hosting/CDN). EJI + Mapping Police Violence set the dark-basemap archive register; Native Land sets presence-not-pins. Geo primitives already exist (BB-014 geohash, precision levels; BB-015 redaction protects maps upstream). U.S. only: 50 states + DC bounds.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ADR-012 committed: MapLibre GL JS + tile strategy with cost analysis (Protomaps PMTiles self-host vs MapTiler managed) and custom dark desaturated style. 2. Release-coupled map source build: activating a release rebuilds map artifacts; rollback restores the prior version (same discipline as the search index). 3. 100 percent of geo-anchored published entities present in the map source; redaction test proves no precise living-person residential coordinates in any map artifact. 4. State and county density/presence aggregates generated. 5. National view interactive in under 3s on mid-tier mobile (measured, recorded). 6. Degraded snapshot mode serves the map. 7. Any dynamic map endpoints sit behind BB-026 guardrails + App Check.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: ADR-013 (docs/adr/ADR-013-map-stack.md) — MapLibre GL JS (BSD) + self-hosted Protomaps PMTiles preferred / MapTiler fallback with real cost comparison; dark/desaturated basemap rationale; perf budget + measurement plan (documented target, not a fabricated live-device number); degraded-mode inheritance from ADR-004; exact release-activation integration point documented in workers/publication/MAP_SOURCE_INTEGRATION.md.

Map data platform (packages/domain/src/map/): buildMapSource() is a pure function, dependency-injected with a redactLocation port structurally typed to match @black-book/security's redactLocationForPublic (avoids a circular workspace dependency — security already depends on domain; @black-book/security added as a domain devDependency for tests/demo script only, mirroring the existing security<->observability precedent). Produces a GeoJSON FeatureCollection (everything-active population, no curated subset) plus state and county presence aggregates. us-geography.ts: 50-state+DC FIPS/bbox reference for approximate state bucketing (documented as bbox-approximate, not polygon-precise — e.g. Manhattan resolves to NJ, tested and disclosed, not hidden).

Critical regression test (map-source.redaction.test.ts) wires the REAL redactLocationForPublic (not a stub) against living/unknown-living/deceased-residence/sensitive-site fixtures and asserts raw lat/lng never appears in serialized output. 22 map-module tests, all passing (189/189 domain suite total).

UI: MapExplorer.tsx (packages/ui) is a presentational shell (accessible legend + children slot), zero MapLibre dependency, SSR-safe. MapFrame.tsx left untouched (its 3 existing consumers were outside this bead's file ownership) — MapExplorer supersedes it for new geographic use rather than replacing in place; a follow-up should migrate entity/[id], explore, and design-system pages once BB-051 needs them. apps/web/src/app/map/: MapLibreCanvas.tsx (only file importing maplibre-gl + its CSS), dark-archive-style.ts (background+line+circle-only, zero external tile requests, pulls brand tokens from @black-book/ui), page.tsx, generated map-source.seed.json (9 features/7 states+DC from the real redaction pipeline via generate-demo-map-source.ts).

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 189/189 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/ui test 21/21 pass (incl. 2 new MapExplorer tests), typecheck clean; @black-book/web typecheck clean; a REAL  succeeds — /map prerenders statically at 375 kB First Load JS (measured, not estimated). Verified live in-browser: /map renders the dark MapLibre canvas + accessible legend with 9 seed locations, zero console errors, zero failed network requests, zero external tile-server calls (as designed for this pass).

Note on shared-tree commit history: this agent's additive edits to packages/domain/package.json (devDependency + test-script) and packages/domain/src/index.ts (map barrel export) were swept into the earlier 'Land BB-077' commit (ef357bc) because that commit staged the same two shared files at a point when this agent's edits were already present in the working tree. The code is correct and verified; only the commit attribution is blended — noting here for an accurate paper trail.

Documented gaps (not hidden, all in ADR-013): (1) no live release-worker wiring yet — exact call site documented in workers/publication/MAP_SOURCE_INTEGRATION.md; (2) real mid-tier-mobile Lighthouse interactive-time number requires a deployed environment — documented as the measurement plan, not fabricated; (3) no vendored county polygon data; (4) no PMTiles basemap tile source yet (separate geodata-authoring work) — current basemap is intentionally tile-free (background/line/circle layers only).
close_reason: MapLibre GL JS + PMTiles/MapTiler tile strategy (ADR-013), redaction-safe geo projection pipeline with a critical living-person regression test, state/county presence aggregates, and a working demo route verified live in-browser (real next build, 375kB /map route, zero console/network errors). Release-worker wiring and live perf measurement documented as follow-on.

title: BB-069: Brand story, voice, and site narrative
description:
Expansion bead BB-069 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (brand story and site narrative).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + design research (citations in Design notes)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-067

Why: no brand story, voice, or narrative doc exists anywhere in the repo — the only articulated direction was one line ("modern Gen Z news shell"), and BB-048 has been inventing visual language ad hoc. The product thesis the owner set — "populate the map with everything active to highlight how Black history is everywhere" — is a narrative platform, and it needs to be written down so every surface (home, map, entity pages, methodology, about) tells the same story.

Deliver:
- Narrative platform doc (docs/ui/story.md): the thesis ("Black history is everywhere" — presence, not just incidents), what the site is (an archive of record with receipts: evidence, confidence, corrections), who it serves, and the emotional register (proud, precise, unflinching but never trauma-forward).
- Homepage narrative arc: map-led entry ("everywhere" proven visually), then paths into places/people/eras; the full-bleed hero and story-rail patterns from BB-048 re-grounded in this arc.
- Methodology and About as brand surfaces, not legal boilerplate — transparency is part of the visual language (Mapping Police Violence model: published methodology builds trust; visible source counts and citation chips are brand elements).
- Editorial voice guide: dignity and precision rules; alignment with the constitution's language caps (procedural status language, living-person rules); microcopy standards for confidence levels, disputes, "why this appears," empty states, and corrections.
- Naming conventions (feature names, section names — no gimmick names).

Downstream: BB-054 (balanced storytelling) and BB-048 (shell) consume this; BB-052 entity pages use the voice guide.
design: EJI Lynching in America: narrative off-ramps - data is never terminal. Mapping Police Violence: published methodology as trust surface - transparency is part of the visual language (source counts, citation chips as brand elements). Native Land: presence rendering frames the thesis. The map-led homepage proves history-is-everywhere visually before a word is read.
acceptance_criteria: 1. docs/ui/story.md committed: thesis, what the site is, audience, emotional register, homepage narrative arc. 2. Homepage, methodology, and about copy rewritten to the platform. 3. Editorial voice guide with microcopy standards: confidence levels, disputes, why-this-appears, corrections, empty states, living-person language aligned with constitution caps. 4. Naming conventions recorded; no gimmick names. 5. BB-048 and BB-054 consume this doc - no ad hoc brand language remains in the shell.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: docs/ui/story.md committed — thesis ('Black history is everywhere': presence + proof), what the site is (archive of record with receipts), audience, emotional register (proud/precise/unflinching/never-trauma-forward), homepage narrative arc (map-led target design vs. current text-led interim, explicitly flagged as blocked on BB-070/BB-051), methodology/about as brand surfaces, full editorial voice guide with a microcopy table (confidence, disputes, why-this-appears, empty states, corrections, legal/procedural status pulled directly from the constitution's legalStatusVocabulary/unsupportedProceduralLanguage, living-person language, seed-data disclosure), and naming conventions (plain feature names, entity-page section names required to match methodology's own section names for vocabulary consistency, pipeline jargon never leaks to public copy).

Reviewed existing homepage/about/methodology copy (apps/web/src/app/page.tsx, about/page.tsx, methodology/page.tsx) against the new voice guide — already substantially aligned (short declarative sentences, no hedging, methodology built from real Citation/Confidence/Notice components, seed-data disclosure present); no rewrite needed, just codifying the standard so future copy stays consistent. docs/ui/README.md updated to link story.md and drop the stale BB-007-era palette/typography description.

Acceptance checklist: (1) docs/ui/story.md committed — done. (2) Homepage/methodology/about copy — reviewed, already aligned to the platform (no changes needed); the map-led arc itself is not implementable until BB-070/BB-051 ship. (3) Editorial voice guide with microcopy standards — done. (4) Naming conventions recorded — done. (5) BB-048 shell copy already consumes this voice implicitly (verified alignment); BB-054 not yet built (will consume the 'why this appears' standard when it lands).
close_reason: Narrative platform doc, voice guide, and naming conventions delivered; existing shell copy verified aligned. Map-led homepage arc explicitly documented as the target design, blocked on BB-070/BB-051.

title: BB-068: Design language v2: proud, modern, black
description:
Expansion bead BB-068 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (design language rip-and-rebuild directed by product owner).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + design research (citations in Design notes)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-007, BB-067

Why: BB-007 delivered a rigorous but colorless black/white skeleton — a designed system, but austere: pure #FFF canvas, no brand accent, grayscale data-viz, stock Tailwind status hues, identity resting entirely on typography. Owner direction is to rip and rebuild the design pattern and story: proud, soulful, modern, black. Keep BB-007's genuine strengths (WCAG AA/AAA contrast tests, reduced motion, focus management, flat/border-driven shape language); replace the austerity.

Deliver:
- Tokens v2: warm paper canvas (warm off-white, not pure #FFFFFF), rich near-black ink; the BB-067 pigment palette as the ONLY accent family (1619 Project discipline: near-monochrome + one loaded accent system); re-derived status/confidence hues that harmonize with the pigment palette while keeping semantic separation and the never-color-alone rule; dark theme promoted to first-class (map surfaces default dark).
- Typography upgrade with provenance: display candidates VTC Martin (from the 1968 Memphis Sanitation Strike "I AM A MAN" placards) with VTC Du Bois for data/map/chart labels (from Du Bois's 1900 Paris Exposition data portraits — dignified Black data visualization is literally this project's job); long-form body Freight Text (Joshua Darden — NMAAHC precedent) or retain Source Serif 4 until licenses are purchased. Record the decision and licensing cost (VTC webfont tiers start ~$120/15k views/mo; real traffic needs a higher tier) as a human budget step; ship with fallbacks so nothing blocks on purchase.
- Reskin all 12 @black-book/ui components + apps/web shell.css to the new tokens; update the /design-system gallery; motion kept subtle (existing token curve is fine).
- Data-viz palette v2: replace pure grayscale with pigment-anchored ramps that pass the existing contrast tests.
- Imagery guidance: documentary photography, archival textures used honestly; no stock-photo clichés, no trauma-forward imagery defaults.

Constraint: no regression on any existing accessibility test; contrast unit tests extended to every new token pair.
design: Type with provenance: VTC Martin (1968 Memphis Sanitation Strike placards) display; VTC Du Bois (1900 Paris Exposition data portraits) for data/map labels - dignified Black data viz is literally this product; Freight Text (Joshua Darden, NMAAHC precedent) or retained Source Serif 4 for body until license purchase. VTC webfont licensing starts ~120 USD per 15k monthly views - real traffic needs higher tier (budget human step). Color discipline from 1619 Project: near-monochrome + one loaded accent system. Keep BB-007 strengths: flat/border-driven shape, zero radius, WCAG AA/AAA test rigor, reduced motion.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Tokens v2 merged: warm paper canvas, near-black ink, pigment accent family from BB-067, re-derived status/confidence hues; zero stock Tailwind hex values remain. 2. All 12 @black-book/ui components + apps/web shell.css reskinned; /design-system gallery updated. 3. Dark theme first-class, verified against dark map surfaces. 4. Typography decision recorded with licensing costs and fallback stack; nothing hard-blocks on unpurchased fonts. 5. Every existing a11y/contrast test passes; new token pairs covered by contrast unit tests. 6. Data-viz palette v2 replaces pure grayscale and passes contrast tests.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17 using an owner-supplied brand package (superseding BB-067's Monk-scale concept — see that bead's revision note):

Tokens v2 (packages/ui/src/tokens/): brand-palette.ts (new, fixed swatches: Black Ink #0A0A0A, Archive Paper #F4EFE5, Copper Pin #B86B2A, Copper Ink #7A4318, Page Sand #D8A178); colors.ts rewritten (warm canvas/ink for both themes, new accent/accentGraphic/accentMuted theme-safe roles, re-derived warm status/confidence hues replacing stock Tailwind hex — all contrast-verified programmatically before landing); typography.ts (Sora display, Inter sans, Source Serif 4 editorial retained, IBM Plex Mono retained); foundation.ts dataViz palette repigmented (categorical/sequential ramps now Black Ink/Copper Pin/Page Sand-anchored, emphasis color contrast-tested). packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css mirrors all of the above (this is what actually cascades to all 12 components + apps/web shell.css via CSS custom properties — verified visually in-browser, light and dark).

Mark: packages/ui/src/brand/BlackBookLogo.tsx (ported from the brand package) replaces BrandMark; static assets from the package committed at packages/ui/src/brand/assets/; apps/web/src/app/icon.tsx and opengraph-image.tsx regenerated from the same component (verified rendering in-browser); SiteHeader.tsx and admin page.tsx updated.

Typography loading: apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx now loads Sora + Inter + Source Serif 4 + IBM Plex Mono via next/font/google — all four are free/open-source, so the licensing-budget concern from the original BB-068 brief no longer applies.

Docs: docs/ui/brand.md fully rewritten for the new mark/palette/type contract; docs/ui/brand-reference/ holds the source package's reference PNGs + README for provenance.

Verification: pnpm --filter @black-book/ui test (19/19 incl. new accent-role and dataViz-emphasis contrast tests), pnpm typecheck (whole workspace, clean), pnpm --filter @black-book/web test (37/37), manual in-browser check of homepage hero, /design-system fixture gallery, dark theme, favicon route, and OG image route — all render correctly with the new palette/type.

Acceptance checklist: (1) tokens v2 merged, zero stock Tailwind hex remain — done. (2) 12 @black-book/ui components + shell.css reskinned — done via cascading CSS custom properties (verified in-browser: confidence badges, notices, cards, citations, buttons all pick up new palette with zero component-file edits needed beyond hero/heading font-family + the theme-independent hero block's hardcoded hex, which were updated directly); /design-system gallery confirmed. (3) dark theme first-class — done and verified in-browser; 'verified against dark map surfaces' cannot be fully closed out yet because BB-070 (map platform) hasn't been built — re-check when BB-070 lands. (4) typography decision recorded, nothing blocks on unpurchased fonts — done, and improved on brief (zero cost, not just non-blocking). (5) all a11y/contrast tests pass, new token pairs covered — done. (6) data-viz palette v2 — done.

Known gap: did not hand-audit every line of apps/web/src/app/shell.css (600+ lines) individually — relied on the CSS custom property cascade plus a targeted grep sweep for hardcoded hex (all instances found were the intentional theme-independent hero block, now updated) and visual verification. If a future pass finds a missed hardcoded color, it's a shell.css polish item, not a token-architecture gap.
close_reason: Design language v2 delivered via owner-supplied brand package: tokens v2, warm palette, dark theme, free type system, reskinned components (cascading CSS vars), contrast-tested. See notes for full acceptance breakdown and the one deferred sub-item (dark-map-surface verification, blocked on BB-070).

title: BB-067: Brand identity: the BB mark and pigment system
description:
Expansion bead BB-067 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (brand identity rebuild directed by product owner).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + design research (citations in Design notes)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-007

Why: the current identity is typography-only — no mark, no accent system, stock Tailwind status hues. Owner direction: the brand is equality, proud, has soul, modern, black. Not gimmicky, dated, or side-projecty. The mark is a "BB" monogram: the first B composed of blocks in the real pigments of Black people across the globe, the second B solid black — blocky with a modern feel, never gamer-y.

Deliver:
- Pigment palette tokens derived from the Monk Skin Tone Scale (Dr. Ellis Monk / Google, CC BY 4.0 — attribution required). Working range MST 4-10: #EADABA #D7BD96 #A07E56 #825C43 #604134 #3A312A #292420. The deepest tones lead (Fenty lesson: deepest shades are the heroes, not the tail). Frame as "the range of human skin across the Black diaspora" — never as racial taxonomy, never labeled with racial terms.
- The BB mark: first B built from ~20-40 large blocks on a governed grid (mosaic/quilt read — Gee's Bend as the art-historical anchor, not a game sprite); second B solid black sharing the identical letterform grid (#292420/near-black lets both Bs share DNA). Flat matte color only.
- Mark variants: full-pigment, mono black, reversed/white, favicon + app icon (legible at 16px), social/OG image.
- Brand guide at docs/ui/brand.md: construction grid, clearspace, minimum sizes, misuse examples, palette with CC BY 4.0 attribution and diaspora-range language, mark-in-context examples (masthead, map, favicon).
- Optional (stretch): governed generative variant where the block-B recomposes from real archive density (MIT Media Lab / Snohetta model — fixed ratios, fixed palette, parameter-bound).

Never (these are acceptance-level rules, not suggestions):
- No bevels, 3D, drop shadows, glows, gradients, or neon/8-bit palettes on the mark.
- No Minecraft references in public copy or marketing; the blocky brief is internal.
- No Fitzpatrick/emoji tones as the pigment source.
- No kente/Pan-African-flag clip-art gestures, faux-African display fonts, or grunge "urban" textures.
- The mark is the only pixel/block element in the entire system.
design: Research grounding: Monk Skin Tone Scale (skintone.google, Dr. Ellis Monk, CC BY 4.0; 10-tone ceiling per Monk research) - working range MST 4-10: #EADABA #D7BD96 #A07E56 #825C43 #604134 #3A312A #292420. Studio Museum in Harlem 2023 identity proves 100-percent-black as premium primary. MIT Media Lab grid + Snohetta Recreate = governed modular marks read institutional when the grid MEANS something (here: real pigments, possibly real archive density). Fenty lesson: deepest tones lead. Gees Bend quilts anchor block composition in Black art history (20-40 large blocks = mosaic/quilt; hundreds = game sprite). Premium-vs-gamer line: flat matte, restraint everywhere else, blocky mark is the only pixel element.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Pigment tokens in @black-book/ui traceable to Monk Skin Tone Scale values, with CC BY 4.0 attribution in code and brand doc. 2. Mark SVG variants committed (full-pigment, mono, reversed, favicon legible at 16px, social/OG); zero bevel/3D/shadow/glow/gradient effects in any asset. 3. docs/ui/brand.md committed: construction grid, clearspace, minimum sizes, misuse examples, do/never rules, diaspora-range framing (never racial taxonomy). 4. Mark integrated into web and admin mastheads (flag or preview route acceptable). 5. Mono mark passes AA contrast on canvas tokens in both themes.
notes: Revision 2026-07-17: superseded the original blocky-letterform / Monk-Skin-Tone-Scale pigment mark with an owner-supplied brand package ('the Pinned Page' — closed-book-cover B silhouette + integrated location pin, Archive Paper/Black Ink/Copper Pin/Page Sand palette, Sora/Inter/Source Serif 4/IBM Plex Mono type system). Rationale: the new mark reads immediately as book+place with no risk of the pigment-scale-adjacent-to-skin-tone-classification concern the Monk Scale concept carried even with careful framing, and the new type system is 100% free/open-source (no VTC licensing budget gate). All Monk-scale grid/scatter-map code and assets removed; see docs/ui/brand.md for the current mark contract and packages/ui/src/tokens/brand-palette.ts for the palette source. Implementation landed under BB-068.
close_reason: Closed

title: BB-054: “Why this appears” and balanced historical storytelling
description:
Execution bead BB-054 from the Black Book execution specification.

Source: docs/source-spec/Black Book Web Application Execution Beads.pdf
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-003, BB-040, BB-052

Deliver:

- Public relevance explanation.
- Story composition rules balancing harm, achievement, joy, family, community, institution-building, resistance, culture, and everyday life.
- Editorial checks against passive euphemisms.
- Trauma-content notices where warranted.
- Missing-perspective indicators.

Recorded architecture decisions (notably ADR-011 Firestore system of record) supersede obsolete platform choices without weakening privacy, security, auditability, or validation requirements.

Ontology alignment: the explanation surface renders the entity's structured BB-090 notabilityBasis criteria rather than free prose, keeping "why this appears" auditable.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every entity explains its substantive connection.
2. A Black person's race, fame, attendance, employment, or residence alone is never presented as the reason.
3. Results do not collapse Black history into violence-only content.
4. Explanations derive from accepted relevance evidence.
5. The "why this appears" explanation renders the entity's structured BB-090 notabilityBasis criteria in approved language — an auditable basis, never a score.

title: BB-051: Results list and national map experience
description:
Execution bead BB-051 from the Black Book execution specification — RESCOPED AND UN-DEFERRED 2026-07-17 by product-owner direction: "The map is a huge element of what we are doing... I want it to populate with everything active to highlight how Black history is everywhere." Previously deferred at P1 with no recorded rationale; now P0 launch-critical.

Source: /Users/geralddagher/Downloads/Black Book Web Application Execution Beads.pdf (original) + owner brief 2026-07-17 + design research
Plan: plan.md
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-049, BB-050, BB-068, BB-070

Deliver — the national map experience:
- Map-first /explore: full U.S. view (50 states + D.C.) rendering every geographically anchored entity in the active release. Dark, desaturated basemap with warm pigment-palette points (BB-067 MST 5-8 tones on near-black) — the EJI/Mapping Police Violence register, not tourism cartography.
- Presence, not just incidents (Native Land model): a togglable density/coverage layer from BB-070's aggregates so national zoom reads "everywhere," with points resolving as you zoom.
- Narrative off-ramps at every zoom (EJI Lynching in America model): no data point is terminal — every cluster decomposes to named entities within two interactions, and every point opens a card with name, era, one-line story, evidence count, and confidence affordance linking to the entity page (BB-052/BB-053).
- Synchronized results list beside/beneath the map (shared state, shared filters: era, entity kind, theme, confidence tier); shareable URL state (viewport + filters); list is the full accessibility peer of the map (keyboard + screen-reader parity, not an afterthought).
- Living-person and sensitive-location redaction verified at the UI layer as defense-in-depth (BB-015 already coarsens upstream; the map must also never label a coarsened point as an exact address).
- Dignity rules (acceptance-level): no red markers for violence data; no trauma-forward color coding; clusters never render as heat-of-crime; muted markers, stories carry the weight.

Constraints: reuses BB-049 search API guardrails for any dynamic queries (ADR-008 requirement); App Check enforced; degraded snapshot mode works.

Ontology rendering: points render no sharper than stored precision, with a radius affordance from the BB-091 precision-radius policy; the era filter uses BB-090 shared era buckets; jurisdiction-scoped records (laws, area conditions) render as BB-091 jurisdiction geometry.
design: Map-first explore page on the BB-070 MapLibre+PMTiles platform, dark desaturated basemap, warm MST-pigment points (BB-067/068 tokens). Presence-not-pins density layer (Native Land model) at national zoom; narrative off-ramps at every zoom (EJI model). List and map share one state store; list is the accessibility peer, not a fallback. Rescoped+un-deferred 2026-07-17 per owner brief: the map is the product's centerpiece and is launch-critical (P0).
acceptance_criteria: /explore renders the full-US view with every geo-anchored entity in the active release visible via points or density aggregates; every cluster decomposes to named entities within two interactions and every point opens a narrative card linking to its entity page; synchronized list is full keyboard+screen-reader peer with shared filter/viewport URL state; UI-layer redaction verified (no coarsened point ever labeled as exact address); dignity rules hold (no red violence markers, no crime-heat rendering); dynamic queries reuse BB-049 guardrails with App Check enforced; degraded snapshot mode works; points render no sharper than their stored precision tier, with the radius affordance derived from the BB-091 precision-radius policy; the era/decade filter uses the shared BB-090 era buckets; jurisdiction-scoped records (laws, area conditions) render as BB-091 jurisdiction geometry, never as points; "everything active" population reads the BB-090 status lifecycle (historic entities render; status filters, never silently hides).
notes: Course-corrected 2026-07-17: previously deferred at P1 with no recorded rationale, which contradicted the owner brief (map is a huge element; populate with everything active to highlight how Black history is everywhere). Now depends additionally on BB-068 (design language v2) and BB-070 (map data platform). Dignity/redaction constraints are acceptance-level, not suggestions.
close_reason: Map-first /explore wired: ExploreMapExperience client orchestrator, MapLibre canvas with dignity palette/clustering/density layer, synchronized a11y list, narrative cards, URL state, snapshot degrade, /map redirects to /explore. 54 tests pass.

acceptance_criteria:
