6. Geocode results resolve to BB-091 jurisdiction ids (state + county FIPS; city/place docs created on-demand at first reference); ZIP input is translated to place then discarded — no ZIP reference corpus, per the ZIP-is-modern-input-only guard.
close_reason: Implemented U.S. Census Geocoder adapter (fixtures-first), address normalization, ZIP translate-then-discard, BB-091 jurisdiction-id resolution, browser location consent flow, geocode cache, coarse-only analytics, rate limiting, and manual-search fallback across packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo/**, packages/domain/src/geocode/** (new), apps/web/src/app/locate/**, apps/web/src/lib/geocode/**, and apps/web/src/components/location/** (new). All 6 acceptance criteria verified by 86 passing tests (42 in packages/domain, 44 in apps/web) plus clean typecheck in both packages. Filed black-book-bnc (parent barrel/config merges: domain geocode barrel export, package.json test-script wiring, Permissions-Policy geolocation relaxation) and black-book-gs9 (pre-existing packages/ui Button/FilterBar cross-transpile gap discovered during testing) as follow-ups, since both require editing files outside this bead's exclusive-path allowlist.

title: BB-049: Search API and experience
description:
Execution bead BB-049 from the Black Book execution specification.

Source: docs/source-spec/Black Book Web Application Execution Beads.pdf
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-019, BB-025, BB-026, BB-048

Deliver:

- Search by person, institution, school, place, event, neighborhood, city, county, state, and ZIP input.
- Alias and misspelling support.
- Facets by entity type, theme, geography, period, record maturity, and research coverage.
- Cursor pagination.
- Search explanations and matched-name display.
- No protected-address matching.

Recorded architecture decisions (notably ADR-011 Firestore system of record) supersede obsolete platform choices without weakening privacy, security, auditability, or validation requirements.

Facet vocabulary comes from BB-090 (status lifecycle, era buckets) — era/period facets cease to be seed-UI fictions; the guardrail filter allowlist extends to status and era (still allowlist-only).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Results are ranked by relevance and connection strength, not fame alone.
2. Search remains bounded under adversarial input.
3. Sparse records are discoverable.
4. Search results indicate why each item is connected to the place or query.
5. Status and era/decade facets use the BB-090 vocabularies and shared decade bucketing; the filter allowlist extends to status and era (allowlist-only guardrails updated); every published result carries at least one BB-090 notabilityBasis (the inclusion gate holds at the search boundary).
close_reason:
All 5 Deliver items and 5 acceptance criteria met, verified by tests and independent code review (not self-report alone).

Deliver:
- Search by person/institution/school/place/event/neighborhood/city/county/state/ZIP: implemented via packages/domain/src/search/ranking.ts (name/alias/topic/summary matching) + packages/security's existing kind/state filter fields.
- Alias and misspelling support: alias matching (exact/prefix/substring) plus bounded Levenshtein fuzzy matching (distance <=2, queries >=4 chars, tested).
- Facets by entity type, theme, geography, period, record maturity, research coverage: packages/domain/src/search/facets.ts computeFacetCounts; apps/web/src/app/search/page.tsx builds FilterBar options from real counts, replacing the old hardcoded reconstruction/civil-rights seed-UI fiction.
- Cursor pagination: BB-026's encodeSearchCursor/decodeSearchCursor wired through the real route for the first time (proven by a cursor round-trip test); the page itself uses a simpler page-local offset param for its own "next page" link, documented as a deliberate two-mechanism split (signed opaque cursor for external/API callers, plain offset for server-rendered no-JS pagination).
- Search explanations and matched-name display: SearchResultView.matchedText/explanation, rendered in ResultList's meta slot.
- No protected-address matching: search operates on the existing coarse-precision projection fields only; no street/residence field exists anywhere in the search index schema.

Acceptance criteria (all test-verified):
1. Ranked by relevance and connection strength, not fame alone: deterministic tiered ranking (exact > prefix > substring > alias > topic > summary > fuzzy) with relatedCount as a strictly-secondary tiebreak, never overriding text relevance — tested.
2. Bounded under adversarial input: BB-026's evaluateSearchQueryGuardrails now has its first real caller; route.test.ts proves SQL-injection-shaped and wildcard-only queries are rejected through the actual HTTP path, closing the "guardrail built, never wired" gap this bead specifically targeted (same class of gap BB-048 found in sanitizeClientErrorDisplay).
3. Sparse records are discoverable: ranking never filters by richness, only orders by it — tested with a thin-summary record.
4. Results indicate why each item connects to the query: buildExplanation() produces a factual sentence per result, no numeric score ever exposed.
5. Status/era facets use BB-090 vocabularies; filter allowlist extended (packages/security's SearchFilterField gained 'status'|'era'); every indexed result carries >=1 notabilityBasis, enforced as defense-in-depth in buildPublicSearchIndexDocs since the gate's originally-intended call site (packages/domain/src/publication/) still doesn't invoke it — a pre-existing gap this bead routes around rather than closes, recorded as a known follow-up.

Execution: dispatcher -> construct scoping pass (read-only, orchestration_run non-functional in this solo deployment, third confirmation this session) resolved the route-topology and guardrail-wiring unknowns before code was written. 3 tranches: domain search module (opus, sequential foundation) -> API route+guardrails and frontend page wiring (opus + default tier, parallel) -> parent-authored shared snapshot-index adapter written first to avoid an ownership conflict between the parallel pair.

Validation: 639 domain + 145 firebase + 80 security + 88 web tests, typecheck, eslint --max-warnings 0, pnpm validate:boundaries all green. pnpm --filter @black-book/web build succeeds (/search/api is a real dynamic route). Verified via curl against both next dev and a next start production build that the server response contains real results; a "stuck on loading" artifact seen in the automated preview-browser tool during next dev only was isolated to that tool's handling of Next's dev-mode streaming boundary-swap script (confirmed absent under production build) -- not a code defect.

Explicit scope boundary: no live Firestore publicSearchIndex reader was built. Route and page both operate on an in-memory snapshot adapted from the seed catalog (apps/web/src/lib/search/snapshot-search-index.ts), matching this app's existing resolvePublicEntity (BB-022) snapshot-first posture. A live reader is a documented future seam plugging into the same PublicSearchIndexDoc contract -- BB-050 (geo discovery) and BB-052 (entity pages) are where live-data wiring happens next, consistent with BB-048's precedent of not building live Firestore consumption ahead of the beads that actually own it.

acceptance_criteria: All Deliver and Acceptance requirements in BB-048 of the source PDF are met, validated, and reflected in plan.md and durable project documentation.
close_reason:
Closed the one real, PDF-scoped gap left on BB-048's own Deliver list ("Error, empty, loading, offline, and degraded states"):

- error.tsx now renders through the shared BB-022 sanitizeClientErrorDisplay helper (apps/web/src/lib/runtime-hardening/error-surface.ts) instead of its own ad-hoc digest-only branch — production never leaks raw error messages, dev/staging shows a safe truncated message. Previously this helper had zero callers anywhere in apps/web (verified via grep).
- New DegradedModeNotice component (apps/web/src/components/DegradedModeNotice.tsx) wires isPublicReadApiDisabled() (also BB-022, also previously uncalled outside its own test) into a real shell-wide banner in SiteShell, alongside the existing client-side OfflineNotice. Operators flipping PUBLIC_READ_API_DISABLED=1 now get a real visible "showing snapshot data" notice, not just a passing unit test.
- loading.tsx, not-found.tsx, and client-side OfflineNotice (navigator.onLine) were already implemented from the prior shell-polish pass and needed no changes.
- Fixed a latent cross-transpile "React is not defined" bug in StatusPage.tsx (used by error.tsx, loading.tsx, not-found.tsx) — same class of bug already fixed in packages/ui/Notice.tsx and apps/web's BB-095 banner components (jsx:"preserve" + node/tsx test loader falls back to classic transform).
- Validated: pnpm --filter @black-book/web test (66/66 pass, incl. 4 new tests), typecheck, build, eslint --max-warnings 0, and pnpm validate:boundaries all green. Manually verified in-browser (homepage + entity page render clean, no console errors).

Scope explicitly NOT included here, and correctly not BB-048's job:
- Live public projection / search / entity data (still seed-fixture reads from public-seed.ts). BB-019's release-activation model, Firestore rules, and typed path helpers (publicActiveRelease/publicEntity in packages/firebase/src/firestore/paths.ts) already exist and are ready to consume — no new backend design needed. Wiring them into apps/web pages is BB-049 (search API) and BB-052 (entity pages, which already has its own AC6 explicitly forbidding hand-authored seed data) — filing a duplicate bead for this would violate the project's "reuse before parallel systems" principle.
- BB-093 (history graph browse UI) is P1 and its own bead text already says it can land post-beta without blocking launch, despite being a tracker dependent of BB-048.

Analysis for this close was done via a two-stage dispatcher -> construct routing pass (construct ran read-only analysis with full file:line citations; no code changes came from that pass). Implementation was small enough (2 files touched, 1 new component, cross-cutting React-import bugfix) that I did it directly rather than spinning up a separate execution agent.

description: Owner: map entity circles must be color-coded the same as KindBadge / dignity-style / kind-encoding already used on NarrativeCard. Do NOT invent a parallel palette. title: Quiet entity links on every list surface
description:
Owner directive: all entities when listed (including within other entities/facts) should be linked, without noisy UI. Prefer inherit-color / underline-on-hover or a short note inviting clicks to learn more — not pill CTAs or copper wash on every name.

Deliver: (1) Shared EntityLink (or equivalent) used wherever entity names appear in lists. (2) Fact subjects show display names not raw ids. (3) Related fallback never shows raw ids when a display name can be resolved. (4) Soft discovery hint near related rails. (5) Keep WCAG: links still identifiable without relying on color alone (hover underline + focus ring). Acceptance: entity pages, fact subject lists, and related rails use quiet links; visual noise does not increase vs current story-rail.

close_reason: Rules deny anonymous client reads of publicReleases entities + publicSearchIndex; rules tests updated. HUMAN: deploy with firebase deploy --only firestore:rules from infra/firebase.

title: Fix unbounded/N+1 live Firestore public entity reads
description: Entity pages re-list entire publicReleases/{id}/entities after a single get (~2N reads per page). listPublicEntityProjections has no limit. Cuts ~1M/day spikes from local+SSG against prod. Keep explore full-list intentional but cached per-request; fix entity path to not full-scan for neighbors.
close_reason: Entity live path now point-gets related+2-hop neighbors only; full release scan reserved for list/explore and request-cached.

title: Single deterministic release builder: full projections, no web-side reconstruction
description: Root cause of the documented-first/0-sources and seed-fallback bugs: projections are too thin so web adapters reconstruct canonical info. NUANCE from validation: publicEntityProjectionSchema (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts:830-911) ALREADY defines claims (full publicClaimProjectionSchema with citations) and notabilityLabels — but the actual projection built by packages/security/src/serialize.ts:43-57 omits claims, notabilityBasis, researchCoverage, and verification state. Build ONE release builder that emits, per entity, from canonical data only: entity projection with published claim views + public citations, source-lineage summary, structured notabilityBasis with evidenceRefs, related-entity projections, map features (GeoJSON from entityLocations), search doc, facts, revision + verification metadata. Wire the publication gates bead into it. Projection generation fails closed when referenced claims/evidence/entities/topics/jurisdictions/locations do not resolve. Public source counts computed at release creation, never at page render. Search, map, facts, and entity pages all read the same release. Then delete web-side synthesis/fallback paths (snapshot-search-index synthesis, entity adapters synthesis, map-projection seed merge).
acceptance_criteria: One builder module with deterministic output + fail-closed reference resolution; projections carry claims/citations/notabilityBasis/researchCoverage/verification metadata; all four public surfaces read the same release; web adapters contain zero reconstruction logic; reconciles types.ts schema with security serializer.
close_reason: Built packages/domain/src/publication/release-builder.ts: buildReleaseEntityArtifacts produces real notabilityBasis (one record per claim predicate, evidenceIds from that predicate's actual cited claims), researchCoverage (from real claim/citation counts), and revision metadata, computed once at publish time. Wired into publish-national-catalog.ts, replacing ~150 lines of inline proxy logic. Fail-closed on unresolvable topicIds/evidenceIds. Web read paths (map-projection.ts, snapshot-search-index.ts, entity adapters) prefer real notabilityBasis, falling back to label-synthesis only for pre-builder seed fixtures. DRY_RUN: 515/515 clean. Left the two-independent-lineages high-impact-predicate gate as an explicit TODO -- 0/1031 claims carry archival metadata, a systemic gap not fixable by relaxing the gate.

title: Controlled topic taxonomy + tag field split (topicIds, mentionedEntityIds, keywords, campaignIds)
description: Replace free-string topicTags with four separated concepts per the validated review: topicIds (controlled historical-theme taxonomy — the ONLY globally facetable field), mentionedEntityIds (people/places/orgs/laws/events referenced — resolvable entity ids, never facets), keywords (search recall only), campaignIds (ingestion/research campaign membership, internal only). Ship a versioned topic taxonomy registry in packages/domain (the only controlled vocab today is legal-only LEGAL_TOPICS at packages/domain/src/legal/types.ts:30-38). Migrate existing topicTags across canonical entities, national-catalog fixtures, projections, and search index; map each current tag to a topicId, an entity mention, or a keyword. Facet builders (map-experience/filters.ts, search/facets.ts) consume topicIds only. Completes the interim allowlist from the P0 facet bug.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy registry with stable ids + labels + schemaVersion; schemas carry the four fields; migration script maps every existing tag; facets read topicIds only; publish schema rejects unknown topicIds.
close_reason: Built controlled topicIds taxonomy (181 ids) in packages/domain/src/taxonomy/, migrated all 12 national-catalog fixtures (1600 tags->topicIds, 58->mentionedEntityIds), updated both facet builders to read topicIds first with a topicTags+allowlist fallback. Required extracting geography/display-radius.ts out of precision.ts and a map-source subpath export to break a circular dependency. Verified: domain 1009/1009, firebase 163/163, web 436/436 tests; DRY_RUN clean on all 515 entities.

title: Remove render-time seed enrichment and fabricated timestamps from live public records
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/public-data/map-projection.ts merges live Firestore projections with bundled seed records at render time (line 138 getPublicEntity + lines 151-199 enrichment), and in the no-seed fallback (lines 200-263) fabricates claims: [], researchCoverage minimal/partial (line 243), timeline: [] (246), and generatedAt/recordUpdatedAt via new Date().toISOString() (249-250). Invariants to enforce: production live records render only from release data; no runtime-generated timestamps standing in for record revision time; no researchCoverage value computed at render time; no seed fallback enriching a live record. Interim (before the release-builder bead lands): surface missing fields as explicitly unknown rather than fabricated. Final state depends on projections carrying claims/citations (release-builder bead).
acceptance_criteria: grep shows no new Date()/Date.now() producing public record revision/generated timestamps in apps/web read paths; live records never merge bundled seed content; researchCoverage comes from the release document only.
close_reason: Removed seed-enrichment merge and fabricated new Date() timestamps from map-projection.ts. Live projections render only their own data now. Fixed the resulting rough edges: sitemap.ts no longer emits an empty lastmod, entity page shows 'Not yet tracked' instead of a blank revision field.

title: Upgrade Next to clear DEP0169 url.parse overlay
description: Upgrade Next.js (and related) so Node DEP0169 url.parse deprecation no longer surfaces on ExplorePage.

title: Consistent multi-source citation display pattern
description:
## Summary
Data/metrics UI repeats heavy SOURCE blocks (often the same link under every figure). Need a consistent pattern for one or many citations without redundancy clutter.

## Acceptance
- Shared citation/source display pattern for single and multiple sources
- Deduplicate identical sources in a figure group
- Apply on data page hate-crime (and similar) surfaces
close_reason: Shared SourceFootnote + DataStatStrip: group footer when sources match, compact extras when unique; applied on /data

title: Entity card click opens entity page
description: Clicking an individual entity card should navigate to /entity/[id], not only select on the map.

title: Uniform entity cards + confidence labels; verify search ties to entity pool
description:
## Summary
Entity cards need uniform layout (confidence wraps); confidence label should be just high/medium/low when a Confidence field title is present; search and related pages may not use the full entity pool.

## Acceptance
- Uniform card layout for available fields
- Confidence wraps; short labels when field titled
- Search/pages use correct entity pool
close_reason: Uniform labeled entity cards; short confidence when titled; search notability synthesis for pool parity; sitemap/history/static params use listPublicEntityViews

title: Map streets, kind/confidence color coding, responsive light+dark
description:
## Summary
Map needs street layers; confidence green→orange by tier; expanded kind colors (person copper, law blue, epicenters gold, massacres red, plantations black, events differentiated); full responsive + dark/light mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Explore/hero map shows street/road detail
- [ ] Confidence uses green (high) → orange (low) with glyph retained
- [ ] Kind palette expanded per product request (copper/blue/gold/red/black/etc.)
- [ ] Surfaces work in light and dark themes
- [ ] Layout remains responsive across breakpoints
acceptance_criteria: Streets on map; confidence green-orange; expanded kind colors; light+dark; responsive
close_reason: Streets via OpenFreeMap; confidence green→orange; expanded kind/semantic tones; light+dark map plate + themed instruments; responsive panels

title: Surface pass: spacing, type weight, map legibility, source URLs, confidence weight
description:
## Summary
Second surface polish after repo-2638150e: pages still feel cramped; headings/body too large/heavy (wall of text); hero map text hard to read; source links point at API endpoints; confidence/status need color+weight.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loosen spacing further across document pages and list/result surfaces
- [ ] Reduce heading weight and body/lede size so pages do not read as wall of text
- [ ] Hero/map overlay text remains readable over map
- [ ] Public source links point at owning-body pages, not raw API endpoints
- [ ] Confidence and similar indicators have dignified color + weight (glyph still present; not color-only)
acceptance_criteria: Spacing/type hierarchy improved; map text readable; source links human; confidence has color+weight
close_reason: Surface pass: lighter type, looser spacing, deeper hero scrim, owning-body source URLs with display remap, confidence/status copper-stone weight.

title: UI polish: spacing, headings, data viz, map slide-out, entity page
description:
## Summary
Polish customer-facing web UI based on review of home/explore, data, map slide-out, and entity pages.

## Why
Spacing between core elements is too tight; non-hero page headings are too large; data page shows counts without meaningful visualization; map entity slide-out and entity detail pages need cleaner visual indicators and color coding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loosen vertical rhythm between core page sections without bloating the hero
- [ ] Reduce non-hero / page heading sizes; keep hero H1 scale
- [ ] Data page visualizes available information (maps/plots/charts), not just numbers
- [ ] Map entity slide-out is clean with visual indicators and dignified color coding
- [ ] Individual entity page is cleaner and consistent with brand
- [ ] Brand/dignity rules preserved (flat matte, copper accents, confidence not color-only, no red/alarm)
acceptance_criteria: Spacing loosened; non-hero headings reduced; data page has meaningful viz; map slide-out and entity page polished with visual indicators; brand/dignity preserved
close_reason: Superseded follow-up is black-book-vm7

Parker House, Southern University) corrected and republished to rel_seed_001. title: Visit block: map hand-off, directions, and visitable status on every record
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §5 'Visit hand-off' + §6 entity row).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('users may be using this to visit places they find here'; 'where possible should link to appropriate map tool').
Size: M
Dependencies: none (works on today's canvas; street basemap w72 improves context).

Why: the hand-off from record to real world is the product's payoff moment. A reader who found the Rosa Parks Museum must reach turn-by-turn navigation in one tap — and must never navigate to an empty lot expecting a building.

Deliver:
- Visit block on /entity/[id] (above the fold on mobile) and on explore narrative cards: 'Open in Maps' + 'Get directions' via geo: URI with Apple Maps and Google Maps web fallbacks (user-agent-appropriate ordering), built ONLY from the record's public-precision geoAnchor.
- Precision guard, fail-closed: redacted/coarse records hand off at locality/county centroid with honest labeling ('approximate area — precise site not published'); a unit test proves no sub-precision coordinate can reach a hand-off URL (extends the public-numeric/precision policy test pattern).
- Visitable status on the record: standing / rebuilt / demolished / marker-only — derived from the existing statusHistory vocabulary where present (currentEntityStatus), an explicit new status label where not; rendered as a mono-register chip in the visit block; catalog backfill for the 104-entity release (research pass reusing the 2026-07-18 provenance pattern).
- Copper discipline: the visit CTA is a primary action (copper); everything else in the block stays ink registers.

Never: exposing finer-than-public precision through ANY hand-off URL; implying a demolished site is visitable; affiliate/tracking parameters on map links.

Acceptance:
1. Rosa Parks Museum record: one tap reaches Apple/Google directions at the public coordinate, 375px both themes. 2. A locality-precision record hands off at centroid with the approximate label. 3. Precision-guard test fails the build if a redacted coordinate leaks. 4. All 104 release records carry a visitable status with a citation where the status is non-obvious. 5. a11y: links have distinguishing accessible names.

Model routing: fast model for the block + links; the status backfill research rides the established parallel-research pattern.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: Address and place search: one input for records, addresses, and places
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §5 'Address + place search').

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('users need to be able to search addresses').
Size: L
Dependencies: repo-b5a06269 (street basemap makes results legible); reuses repo-40192496's adapter surface (census-geo geocoder, packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo — same client, user-facing this time).

Why: a visitor planning a trip starts from where THEY are ('what's near 125th and Lenox') — the archive must answer place-first questions, not only record-first ones. On mobile this becomes the primary entry into the map.

Deliver:
- One search input (explore panel + /search) with typed result groups: Records (existing search pipeline) and Places (census-geo geocode: addresses, cities, landmarks). Free-first: Census Bureau geocoder, no key; extend provider only if match quality demonstrably fails.
- Selecting a place result flies the camera via the existing presets (no new camera grammar) and shows nearby records within the flown viewport; no permanent pin is dropped.
- Debounced, cancellable requests; graceful zero-state ('No places matched — try a record search'); keyboard + screen-reader parity with the existing result list pattern.
- Geocode responses cached (same never-re-geocode-the-same-query rule as repo-40192496's cache).
- URL state: a place search that flew the camera is shareable via the existing viewport params — no new URL dimension.

Never: sending user queries to a paid geocoder by default; logging raw address queries beyond operational need (privacy posture); a second competing search UI.

Acceptance:
1. Typing '1616 Chappelle St, Tulsa' or 'Sweet Auburn Atlanta' flies to the place with nearby records visible, 375px and desktop, both themes. 2. Record search behavior unchanged and same-speed. 3. Zero-result and error states render the Notice pattern. 4. Repeat queries hit the cache (verified by request count). 5. A11y: results navigable by keyboard, announced count.

Model routing: fast model — well-specified integration against an existing adapter; escalate only if provider-quality judgment is needed.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: Black population by county by decade: census data model, ingestion, and map layer
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: pull population data at least by decade — Black population by county, decade over decade — and model the relationship between population flows (Great Migration) and entities/events. Think holistically; model data consistently across datasets.

REUSE: county join key = 5-digit FIPS already on /geo/us-counties-20m.geojson properties (stateFips+countyFips, landed 2026-07-18) and on MapCountyAggregate (packages/domain/src/map/map-source.ts); decade machinery = deriveEraBuckets (packages/domain/src/era.ts) + per-decade graph views (graph/decades.ts); presence-not-incidents dignity framing + tier philosophy = apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/density.ts (no false-absence tier) + DENSITY_TIER_FILL (dignity-style.ts); ingestion provenance = RetrievalEvent/SourceCapture (contentHash) + source registry + drift tracking (packages/domain/src/adapters/registry.ts, drift.ts); operator lane = operator-cli subcommand + thin skill (BB-085).

Data model: demographics county-decade docs {fips5, stateFips, countyFips, decade, totalPopulation, blackPopulation, source, retrievedAt, contentHash} — public census statistics (counts are fine; the non-numeric policy bans entity notability/relevance scores, not published government statistics — record that reading in the schema doc comment). One collection, keyed {fips5}_{decade}, idempotent re-ingest via contentHash.

Sources (free-first): NHGIS/IPUMS API for machine-readable historical decennial county tables 1790–2020 (needs free account + key — see companion HUMAN bead); modern slice available NOW keyless via api.census.gov decennial P.L. tables (2000/2010/2020 race by county) to prove the pipeline end-to-end before NHGIS lands.

Map/UI (follow the 6-step filter-extension recipe: url-state.ts → filters.ts applyExploreFilters → buildExploreFacetOptions → ExploreMapFeatureProperties → feature enrichment → parse/serialize): county choropleth mode on the existing density layer with a decade control tied to the era filter; joins to the county polygons already on the canvas (repo-b91695d2 county lines layer). Holistic tie-in: decade graph views + entity eraBuckets let the UI show entities/events active in the same decade as the population frame (Great Migration flows beside Bronzeville/Harlem/Greenwood records). Respect BB-051 dignity rules: population presence is the story, never deficit framing.
notes: 2026-07-18 session: DATA + INGESTION portion DONE (map layer still open). Production black-book-efaaf now has censusCountyDecades (9,661 docs = 2000/2010/2020 × ~3,220 counties, total+Black population, provenance quartet, contentHash-idempotent; public-read rules). Loader: packages/firebase/src/demographics/load-cli.ts (jurisdictions-loader lane — operator-cli promotion boundary forbids direct writes). Fixed adapter bug: 2020 blackAloneVariable was P1_003N (White alone), now P1_004N. ALSO landed: ACS 5-year 2024 county+tract collections (acs-load-cli.ts), Opportunity Atlas + HOLC ingests (scripts/), external-data-sources.ts registry (19 sources), CENSUS_API_KEY in Secret Manager. Remaining for this bead: county choropleth decade control per the 6-step filter recipe.

title: Promote catalog related entries to canonical EntityRelationship records + release graph artifacts
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: get really good at mapping relationships between entities. The FULL pattern already exists — do not invent a parallel one: packages/domain/src/relationship.ts (EntityRelationship, 19 types with RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_SEMANTICS, evidenceIds REQUIRED >=1), graph/build.ts (deterministic release artifacts at publicReleases/{rel}/graph/adjacency/{entityId}, graph/decades/{decade}, graph/all-time), graph/adjacency.ts (evidence-ranked capped PublicRelatedEntry), graph/decades.ts (EraSpan bucketing via deriveEraBuckets).

Today's shortcut (2026-07-18): the national catalog carries inline 'related' entries (22 curated bidirectional links: located_at/occurred_at/related_to — museum↔arrest site, marches↔Pettus Bridge↔Brown Chapel, Harlem↔Apollo/Schomburg/Studio Museum/Abyssinian, Greenwood↔Vernon AME, Sweet Auburn↔Ebenezer/Big Bethel, etc.) written straight into the projection docs. That is the bootstrap path, not the pattern.

Deliver: (1) Convert those links into canonical EntityRelationship records, evidenceIds citing the entities' claim ids. (2) Run graph/build.ts over them to emit the release graph artifacts; entity pages + History edge lines read adjacency artifacts (the reader path source.ts hydrates related neighbors already). (3) A repeatable extraction step for future research waves: relationship candidates from co-located anchors (shared geohash prefix) + shared jurisdiction + claim-text mentions, reviewed via the existing triage skill lane, never auto-published without evidence links. Acceptance: /entity pages for Rosa Parks Museum and Edmund Pettus Bridge show related records from graph artifacts; decades graph views include the new edges.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.
close_reason: Catalog related promoted via extractCatalogRelationships; release-builder relatedEntries; publish-national-catalog writes entityRelationships + graph adjacency/decade/all-time. Offline test: museum+bridge related=1.

title: Location resolution pipeline: census-geo adapter → EntityLocation → projection geoAnchor
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: get really good at validating/approximating locations and associating lat/lng with entities WITHOUT relying on an LLM each time, consistently across the project. REUSE, do not reinvent: the Census Bureau geocoder adapter already exists at packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo (free, no key; matchMethod vocab already includes geocode_census); canonical location records are entityLocationSchema (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts — role, geometry, point, match {method, precision, confidence, recordedAt}, jurisdictionIds); geohash + prefixes via packages/domain/src/geography/geohash.ts / buildGeoPointFields; precision redaction via geography/precision.ts + apps/web geo-precision.ts.

Deliver: (1) operator-cli subcommand 'locate' (thin caller per BB-085, matching packages/operator-cli/src/bin.ts pattern) + .claude/skills/black-book/locate skill wrapper: input entity id + address/place text → census-geo geocode → deterministic validation (state-bbox containment vs jurisdictionLabel using US_STATES from map/us-geography.ts — same check the 2026-07-18 catalog QA script ran) → EntityLocation doc with full match provenance. (2) Geocode result cache keyed on normalized query text so the same address is NEVER re-geocoded (reuse the idempotency-record pattern from firestore/audit-outbox.ts). (3) publish-national-catalog.ts (packages/firebase/scripts) reads EntityLocation when present, catalog lat/lng only as manual_research fallback. (4) Retire apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/entity-geo.ts per its own documented retirement note — live projections now carry geoAnchor (landed 2026-07-18, commit d7c48f6). Acceptance: locating one new entity end-to-end writes a cached, validated, provenance-carrying EntityLocation and the map renders it with zero hand-edited coordinate tables.

title: BB-101: Redesign quality gate: a11y, motion, performance, and brand conformance
description:
Redesign quality gate for the blap experience (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md is the binding spec; brand-pack/ is the binding kit).

Source: owner briefs 2026-07-17 (redesign) + 2026-07-18 (rebrand, full dark/light support, fully responsive, brand-pack scheme adherence).
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-097, BB-098 (in progress); gates their output plus the 2026-07-18 rename/reskin work.

Why: one gate that proves the redesign is actually done — brand-conformant, accessible, both-theme, responsive, performant — instead of per-stream self-assessment.

Deliver — four matrices, evidence committed (screenshots + traces in docs/ui/qa/):
1. BRAND-PACK CONFORMANCE: palette-only colors (5 core + 2 neutrals via tokens, no raw hex in app code); typography = Sora SemiBold headlines / Inter UI / Source Serif 4 editorial / IBM Plex Mono data (Sora landed 2026-07-18 — verify every heading register actually renders Sora); type scale within kit p.2 (H1 56/64 → caption 12/16, fluid between breakpoints); mark usage rules p.3 (clear space, min sizes 120/48/32, no separation/stretch/shadow/recolor, page-band order); copper discipline 10-15% orientation-only.
2. THEME MATRIX: every route in §6's surface inventory rendered light AND dark; AA contrast on every text/control pair in both; theme-invariant surfaces (map plate, footer, on-map header) verified deliberate; no hydration flash.
3. RESPONSIVE MATRIX: every surface at 375/768/1280; no horizontal page scroll; wide content scrolls in-container; touch targets >=44px; map surfaces one-handed usable at 375px (street zooms included once w72 lands).
4. MOTION+PERF: prefers-reduced-motion collapses every animation including camera flights; route-arrival + hero-dissolve within token durations; map cold load + navigation traces recorded; existing a11y test suite green.

Never: signing off from memory — every cell needs an artifact; treating dark theme or 375px as best-effort (owner: P0 findings).

Acceptance: all four matrices complete with zero open P0 findings; failures filed as beads with screenshots; gate re-runnable (scripted screenshot harness preferred over hand capture).

Model routing: fast model + scripted capture for the sweep; strong model only for judgment calls on contrast/copper findings.
design: Mirrors BB-057 for the redesign tranche. Checklists to execute: guide p.13 launch checklist (verbatim), BB-051 dignity/redaction acceptance list, contrast suite across tokens v3, reduced-motion audit of BB-098 camera work, Lighthouse mobile preset on homepage + explore, cross-browser sweep Chrome/Safari/Firefox at 375/768/1280.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every audit executed with recorded evidence (screenshots, traces, filled checklists). 2. Guide p.13 checklist passes 100%. 3. BB-051 dignity/redaction list re-verified on the new map. 4. No open P0/P1 findings from this gate. 5. A11y, contrast, and reduced-motion pass in both themes.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.
