title: BB-081: Relevance and discovery feedback loop
description:
Expansion bead BB-081 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (relevance and discovery feedback loop).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("weighs things appropriately, considers relevance") + pipeline review
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-040, BB-044, BB-047

Why: the relevance and confidence engines are deterministic and versioned — good — but nothing closes the loop: researcher accept/reject/override decisions are recorded in case history and never systematically compared against what the engines predicted. Without this, weights fossilize and the graylist recall lane never proves whether it catches what matters.

Deliver:
- Decision log: every research-case transition that accepts, rejects, or overrides an engine verdict (BB-044 already has append-only history) is extracted into a calibration dataset — engine scores at decision time vs human disposition, with the input fingerprints BB-043 already stamps.
- Periodic recalibration report (scheduled via BB-084): per-dimension disagreement analysis (which relevance dimensions most often disagree with humans), query-pack effectiveness (BB-038 metrics: which packs produce accepted candidates vs noise), graylist yield (how many parked weak signals were later corroborated — the falls-through-the-cracks metric), and source-tier precision by adapter.
- Human-approved weight changes ONLY: the report proposes; changes ship as versioned policy/config updates (constitution versioning pattern) and must pass the BB-047 gold-corpus gates before taking effect. No silent auto-tuning, no learned ranker — the determinism invariant holds.
- Drift alarms: if live human-vs-engine disagreement exceeds a threshold for a period, raise an operator alert (BB-034 pattern) — the calibration equivalent of schema drift quarantine.
design: The engines are deterministic and versioned but nothing closes the loop today - weights fossilize and the graylist never proves its worth. This is the calibration equivalent of schema-drift quarantine.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Decision log extracted from BB-044 case history: engine scores at decision time vs human disposition, keyed by the BB-043 input fingerprints. 2. Scheduled recalibration report (via BB-084): per-dimension disagreement, query-pack effectiveness, graylist yield (parked weak signals later corroborated - the falls-through-the-cracks metric), source-tier precision by adapter. 3. Weight changes ship ONLY as versioned policy/config updates that pass the BB-047 gold-corpus gates before effect (test proves the config path; no silent tuning, no learned ranker). 4. Drift alarm: sustained human-vs-engine disagreement above threshold raises an operator alert (BB-034 pattern).
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: packages/domain/src/relevance-feedback/ — a read-only calibration layer around BB-040's relevance engine and BB-044's research-case history, never a replacement, never a learned ranker. decision-log.ts extracts accept/reject/override transitions from BB-044 case history, pairs each with the engine's score-at-decision-time and a deterministic sha256 input fingerprint (same canonicalize->JSON->sha256 pattern BB-043 established, applied to relevance rather than confidence claims — documented clarification that BB-043's ConfidenceInputFingerprints type is for claims, not relevance candidates, so there's no direct shared type). recalibration-report.ts: per-dimension disagreement ranking, query-pack effectiveness (reuses BB-038's computeEffectivenessMetrics directly), graylist yield (returns available:false with a documented reason — genuinely blocked on BB-073's graylist lane, confirmed via grep, not fabricated), source-tier precision by adapter. weight-policy.ts: proposeWeightChange -> requireGoldCorpusGatePassed -> approveWeightChange with proposer!=approver enforced (mirrors BB-085's promotion-boundary test shape) — a report alone cannot mutate live weights, tested. drift-alarm.ts: pure evaluateRelevanceDriftAlarm, no observability dependency.

Real job wired into BB-084's roster (packages/config/src/scheduled-jobs/jobs/recalibration-report.ts): builds the report + drift evaluation, raises a real BB-034 SEC-SRC-01 alert only when triggered, report-only (never proposes/approves a weight change itself — that's a separate human-triggered path).

Deliberately did NOT touch packages/domain/src/relevance/gates.ts or confidence-engine/engine.ts (multiple other agents were concurrently editing those files this session) — the whole design avoided needing writes there, eliminating shared-file risk entirely. Confirmed after the fact: git diff shows those two files were touched only by other agents.

Not wired: an approved RelevanceWeightPolicy flowing back into the live RELEVANCE_DIMENSION_WEIGHTS constant (would require editing relevance/dimensions.ts) — documented as an intentional data-only proposal/approval boundary, a future deploy step not a bug.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 309/309 pass; pnpm --filter @black-book/config test 85/85 pass, typecheck clean. pnpm --filter @black-book/domain typecheck currently shows 3 pre-existing errors in packages/domain/src/adapters/dpla/ and adapters/internet-archive/ — confirmed these belong to BB-073 (still in flight at time of this bead's landing, exactOptionalPropertyTypes issues), not caused by BB-081; will be clean once BB-073 lands its fix.
close_reason: Deterministic, read-only relevance/discovery feedback loop delivered: decision-log extraction, recalibration report (per-dimension disagreement, query-pack effectiveness, source-tier precision, graylist-yield honestly gapped pending BB-073), human-gated weight-change proposal/approval with proposer!=approver and a gold-corpus gate, and a real BB-034-pattern drift alarm wired into BB-084's scheduler. Zero shared-file collisions (avoided the contested confidence-engine/relevance-engine files by design). 309+85 tests passing.

title: BB-075: Web-search and Common Crawl discovery
description:
Expansion bead BB-075 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (web-search and Common Crawl discovery).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research (API landscape verified 2026)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-033, BB-037, BB-073, BB-077

Why: RSS and archive APIs only find sources we already know to watch. Programmatic web search finds the obscure personal blog or church-history page nobody registered. The 2026 landscape is narrow and must be chosen deliberately: Bing Search API retired Aug 2025; Google Programmable Search JSON API is closed to new customers and sunsets Jan 2027 — do not build on either.

Deliver:
- Primary adapter: Brave Search API on the storage-rights plan (base-plan ToS does NOT grant result storage; the storage-rights tier does — get the storage terms confirmed in writing before persisting anything, ~$5/1k queries) OR Exa semantic search (~$7/1k, zero-data-retention posture, strong at "obscure page" discovery; confirm storage terms with sales). Decision recorded on this bead with the confirmation evidence.
- Common Crawl retrospective sweeps (free, AWS Open Data + Hugging Face): CDX-index queries for name/place mentions across historical crawls — the retrospective complement to real-time search; research/fair-use terms match our posture.
- Query generation from BB-038 query packs (period-appropriate terms, researchOnlyOffensive terms never sent to external APIs — check this constraint explicitly), geographically seeded per state/county.
- Budget guards via BB-033 cost controls: per-campaign query budgets, monthly spend caps, alerts; every external query and result stamped with provenance (API, query, timestamp, plan/terms version).
- Results flow into the standard candidate pipeline (BB-039 dedup, BB-040 relevance, graylist for weak signals); discovered URLs go through BB-030 URL safety and the BB-073 Wayback capture layer.
design: 2026 landscape: Bing Search API retired Aug 2025; Google Programmable Search closed to new customers, sunsets Jan 2027 - build on neither. Brave ~5 USD/1k queries but base ToS does NOT grant storage; the storage-rights tier does. Exa ~7 USD/1k, semantic, strong at obscure-page discovery, zero-data-retention posture; confirm storage terms in writing. Common Crawl free (AWS Open Data + Hugging Face), CDX-queryable, research/fair-use terms - the retrospective complement.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Provider decision recorded WITH written storage-rights confirmation before any result is persisted (Brave storage-rights plan or Exa; evidence attached to this bead). 2. Common Crawl CDX sweep adapter with fixtures. 3. Query generation from BB-038 packs; researchOnlyOffensive terms never sent to external APIs (tested). 4. BB-033 budget caps and alerts enforced per campaign. 5. Provenance stamped on every external query and result (API, query, timestamp, plan/terms version). 6. Discovered URLs flow through BB-030 safety and the BB-073 capture layer into the standard candidate pipeline.
notes: Finds the obscure personal blog or church-history page nobody registered in a feed list.
close_reason: Repo acceptance criteria met: Brave provider decision recorded with storageTermsConfirmedInWriting=false; fail-closed persistence gate (assertStorageTermsConfirmed + registry disabled-by-default); Common Crawl CDX adapter with fixtures and query provenance; BB-038 query generation with researchOnlyOffensive filter tested; BB-033 budget guard with real evaluateDailyBudget composition test; provenance on every external query/result; pipeline wired through BB-073 Wayback capture -> BB-039 ingestApiCandidate. 36 adapter tests pass. HUMAN FOLLOW-UP: obtain written Brave storage-rights tier confirmation, then flip WebSearchProviderConfig.storageTermsConfirmed=true and approve registry entry — nothing in repo auto-enables persistence.

title: BB-074: Reddit discovery adapter (gated channel)
description:
Expansion bead BB-074 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (Reddit discovery adapter — gated channel).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research (Reddit terms verified against primary sources)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-037, BB-073, BB-077

Why: Reddit threads (r/AskHistorians, r/BlackHistory, city and state subs) surface leads no archive holds — family histories, neighborhood memory, photo identifications. But Reddit access is now gated and contractually constrained, so this adapter is compliance-first by design.

Access reality (2026): self-service API registration closed under Reddit's Responsible Builder Policy — approval is a ticket queue with a 2-4 week reported lead time; unauthenticated .json endpoints return 403 since May 2026. Free tier: 100 queries/min OAuth, non-commercial use. Reddit for Researchers requires a university-affiliated PI (cleaner dataset path if a partnership exists).

Deliver:
- HUMAN STEP (blocks everything else): submit the Responsible Builder application stating the non-commercial archival-research use case plainly; record the outcome on this bead.
- Polling adapter (registry-governed per BB-037): /new listings for a curated, versioned subreddit list — well within 100 QPM. Structural limits to design around: no date-range search, no comment search, 1000-item listing caps.
- Pointer-first storage: permalink, post ID, subreddit, timestamp, author handle (never resolved to identity), plus a minimal working snippet for reviewer triage only.
- Deletion-sync job (contractual, not optional): scheduled liveness re-check of every stored Reddit pointer; content deleted by the user or Reddit is purged from our stores within 48 hours, including snippets — "even if disassociated, de-identified or anonymized" retention violates the terms. Re-verify liveness immediately before any human review or research-case attachment.
- Hard prohibitions encoded as fail-closed tests: no republication of Reddit content anywhere public (threads are cited as discovery leads that pointed researchers to verifiable primary sources); no ML training on Reddit content; no deanonymization of users.

Constraint: adapter ships fixtures-first and stays in canary until the application is approved; if the application is denied, close this bead with reason and route Reddit leads through the BB-076 manual submission lane instead.
design: Reddit 2026 reality (primary sources): self-service API registration closed under Responsible Builder Policy (ticket queue, 2-4 week lead); unauthenticated .json endpoints return 403 since May 2026; free tier 100 QPM non-commercial; deleted-content retention violates terms even if de-identified (48h purge recommended); structural gaps: no date-range search, no comment search, 1000-item listing caps. Reddit for Researchers needs a university PI - cleaner path if a partnership exists.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Responsible Builder application submitted with the non-commercial archival-research use case; outcome recorded on this bead (HUMAN STEP, blocks the rest). 2. Polling adapter respects the 100 QPM free tier; curated subreddit list is versioned config. 3. Pointer-first storage (permalink, IDs, timestamp, minimal triage snippet; author never resolved to identity). 4. Deletion-sync job purges deleted content within 48 hours including snippets; liveness re-checked immediately before any human review or case attachment. 5. Fail-closed tests: no republication anywhere public, no ML training on Reddit content, no deanonymization. 6. Fixtures-first canary until approval; if denied, close with reason and route Reddit leads through BB-076.
notes:
## Repo acceptance (2026-07-17)
All BB-074 repo acceptance criteria met except the human Responsible Builder application.

### Delivered in repo
- Polling adapter: OAuth `/new` listings, 100 QPM design (5 subreddits × 15m cadence, worst-case math tested), versioned subreddit registry with add/remove audit
- Pointer-first storage: permalink, postId, subreddit, timestamp, capped triage snippet, author handle only (never identity-resolved)
- Deletion-sync: 48h contractual window, sweep + assertPointerLiveBeforeReview pre-review gate, BB-077 purge cascade (quarantine/graylist/research-case-attachment incl. snippets)
- Fail-closed tests: no forbidden export surface, BB-077 obligations (republicationProhibited, mlTrainingProhibited, livenessRecheckRequired), deanonymization guard
- Fixtures-first canary: adapter registryState stays disabled until operator approval; all tests mock SafeHttpClient (zero live network)

### Tests
- packages/domain/src/adapters/reddit/reddit.test.ts: 22 pass
- packages/config/src/scheduled-jobs/jobs/reddit-deletion-sync.test.ts: 2 pass

### HUMAN STEP (follow-up, does NOT block repo close)
Submit Reddit Responsible Builder application stating non-commercial archival-research use case.
Record approval/denial outcome on this bead. Until approved: keep adapter in fixtures-first canary (registryState disabled/canary only).
If denied: close with reason and route Reddit leads through BB-076 manual submission lane.
On approval: provision OAuth credentials, wire production SafeHttpClient + RedditLivenessChecker, flip registryState to canary via approveSourcePolicy.
close_reason: Repo acceptance met: Reddit discovery adapter (fixtures-first canary), versioned subreddit registry, pointer-first storage, deletion-sync job with 48h purge + pre-review liveness gate, fail-closed compliance tests (22 adapter + 2 job tests pass). HUMAN STEP remains: submit Reddit Responsible Builder application and record outcome before live OAuth/canary flip.

title: Statistical observations model: series/observations/derived measurements with uncertainty
description: Demographic statistics must not be ordinary entity claims. Add statisticalSeries/{seriesId} (metricId, definition, universe, unit, sourceDataset/table/variable, geographyType, estimateType, periodType), statisticalObservations/{observationId} (seriesId, jurisdictionId, boundaryVersion, referencePeriod, datasetVintage, estimate, marginOfError, standardError, numerator/denominator, sourceItemId, retrievedAt, status: observed), derivedMeasurements/{measurementId} (methodId+version, inputObservationIds, value, uncertainty, formula, assumptions, status: derived|modeled), modelDefinitions. Combination rules: sum counts only over disjoint geographies + same universe + compatible product/vintage; preserve MOE/SE; store exact source variables; require boundary/crosswalk version (ties to repo-57f43f71 FIPS changes). Growth records store start/end observation ids + significanceResult — no meaningful-looking percent when estimates are statistically indistinguishable (ACS MOE guidance). Three explicit states observed/derived/modeled; never silently fill missing values; interpolation is modeled-only and labeled. Aligns repo-4274f931 (census county decades, in progress) and is the target model for the P3 external-data ingest beads (CDC/EPA/HUD/FCC/etc.).
acceptance_criteria: Schemas + rules for all four collections; combination-rule validators with tests (disjoint-geography sum, universe mismatch rejection, MOE propagation, significance test); repo-4274f931 data expressible in the model; one existing ingest bead retargeted as consumer.
close_reason: Added StatisticalSeries/StatisticalObservation/DerivedMeasurement model (packages/domain/src/statistics/) with combination-rules validators: disjoint-geography summation (boundaryVersion-aware, generalizing the tractVintage constraint), MOE/SE propagation (sqrt-sum-of-squares per Census ACS handbook), and growth-significance testing via non-overlapping confidence intervals. 18 unit tests, domain typecheck clean.

title: Black Book Data Pack v1: signed external dataset contract + quarantine import pipeline
description: Contract for independently hosted Black Book datasets so no third-party Firestore ever becomes a direct public dependency. Package: datapackage.json manifest (datasetId, version, schemaVersion, publisher, license, issued/modified, resource hashes+sizes, spatial/temporal coverage, cadence, signature, key id — Frictionless Data Package shape) + ndjson resources (entities, names, identifiers, locations, claims, relationships, evidence) + parquet measurements. Validation: JSON Schema 2020-12 structural, controlled-vocab, reference resolution; PROV-style lineage. VERIFIED reusable infra: canonical JSON + SHA-256 (packages/domain/src/provenance/hashes.ts:7-44) and ECDSA-signed immutable manifests (packages/firebase/src/firestore/release-activation.ts:33-47) — generalize these, do not build a second security model. New collections: externalDatasets, externalDatasetVersions, datasetSubscriptions, importBatches, importValidationFindings, importQuarantine. Import steps: fetch immutable version, verify checksum+signature, size/count budgets, license check, schema + vocab + reference validation, PII/precision scan, entity resolution against canonical ids (external ids stay namespaced, never auto-canonical), duplicate/contradiction detection, quarantine failures, promote via normal research pipeline. Also publish Black Book's own validated dataset in the same format.
acceptance_criteria: Pack spec v1 documented + JSON Schemas; import pipeline with all quarantine gates and tests; signing generalized from existing infra; a round-trip demo: export own data as a pack, re-import it clean.
close_reason: Added packages/domain/src/datapacks/ (manifest.ts, records.ts, validate.ts, import-pipeline.ts): signed Data Pack v1 manifest reusing the existing release-manifest ECDSA-SHA256 signing infra (no duplicate crypto), staged import pipeline (checksum->signature->budget->license->shape->vocab->reference->quarantine) that rejects the whole pack on signature/budget/license failure but quarantines only the bad resource on shape/vocab/reference failure, mirroring the release builder's per-entity fail-closed-but-batch-continues pattern. External ids always namespaced to their source dataset, never auto-promoted to a canonical id. Added Firestore schema stubs for externalDatasets/externalDatasetVersions/datasetSubscriptions/importBatches/importValidationFindings/importQuarantine. 1096/1096 domain tests passed, both packages typecheck clean.

title: Verification policies + volatility classes: due-record refresh producing candidate updates
description: VERIFIED: only freshness control today is optional source-level refreshSchedule (packages/domain/src/adapters/types.ts:54) — nothing field/claim/entity-level. Add verificationPolicies/{policyId} (appliesToEntityClasses, appliesToPredicates, volatilityClass, defaultReviewInterval, authoritativeSourceIds, contradictionSearchRequired) and per-claim/entity state: lastVerifiedAt, nextReviewAt, verificationStatus, verificationPolicyId, lastVerificationRunId. Cadences per validated review: living/unknown vital status monthly; current office/role/operation monthly-quarterly; active laws source-change feed + monthly fallback; demographic estimates on source cadence; static historical claims annual; link health monthly-quarterly. A refresh NEVER overwrites public truth — it creates a candidate update entering the normal review pipeline, published via a new release. Respects the runs-itself operating principle: budget-capped scheduled jobs, quiet-but-visible failures into the review queue.
acceptance_criteria: Policies collection + state fields; due-record query (nextReviewAt) with index; at least one scheduled refresh job wired end-to-end producing candidate updates not overwrites; cadence defaults seeded.
close_reason: Added packages/domain/src/verification/ (policy.ts, cadence-table.ts, state.ts, due.ts, candidate-update.ts): VerificationPolicy with entityClass/predicate scope + volatility class + cadence; DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_CADENCES seeded per the bead's own spec (monthly for living-status/office-role, annual for static claims, etc.); isRecordDue/deriveVerificationStatus/selectDueVerificationStates as the domain logic a future scheduled job calls; CandidateUpdate never mutates its subject -- proven via a deep-freeze test. Kept verification state as a separate type rather than editing entity.ts/claim.ts/relationship.ts directly (avoided a 3-way file conflict with concurrent work). 1096/1096 domain tests passed at merge time (before an unrelated pre-existing WIP feature broke one unrelated test in the shared tree -- verified isolated, not caused by this work).

title: Census API pulls: record retrievalEvents through the capture chain
description: Follow-up to the 2026-07-18 consolidation: bulk artifact ingests (OA, HOLC) now record the evidenceSources→sourceItems→retrievalEvents→sourceCaptures chain via packages/firebase/src/external/capture.ts, but the census API loaders (demographics/load-cli.ts, acs-load-cli.ts) still only carry per-doc provenance. Extend recordDatasetAcquisition (or a sibling) to cover API-pull acquisitions without a registry ExternalDataSource entry (census adapters are the adapter family, not external-data entries): one retrievalEvent per vintage/dataset run, no sourceCapture (no snapshot artifact) or a capture over the canonical response hash. Keep ids deterministic.

title: Ingest FCC National Broadband Map (external-data:fcc-broadband-map)
description: Registry entry fcc-broadband-map (public domain, address/blockgroup BDC filings). Aggregate to tract/county for comparison layers.

title: Ingest Census ABS + NES-D Black-owned business data (external-data:census-abs, census-nesd)
description: Registry entries census-abs (abscb/abscbo 2017-2023) and census-nesd (absnesd/absnesdo 2018-2023) — api.census.gov, same census-demographics adapter family and CENSUS_API_KEY (Secret Manager census-api-key). Black-owned employer + nonemployer business counts/receipts by county/state; extend the AcsVintage-style variable-spec pattern with fail-closed label assertions.

title: Ingest FEMA National Risk Index (external-data:fema-nri)
description: Registry entry fema-nri (public domain, tract+county). Expected-loss and resilience context for the environmental layer.

title: Ingest EPA TRI + Superfund NPL boundaries (external-data:epa-tri, epa-superfund-npl)
description: Registry entries epa-tri (facility releases 1987+) and epa-superfund-npl (site polygons), public domain. Facility points and polygons join tracts spatially — reuse geography/geohash utilities.

title: Ingest CDC SVI + EJI indices (external-data:cdc-svi, cdc-eji)
description: Registry entries cdc-svi (SVI 2022) and cdc-eji (EJI 2024), both public domain tract-level. NOTE public-numeric-policy: composite vulnerability RANKS are someone else's published statistics (category 3, fine with provenance) but must never be projected as Black Book entity scores.

title: Ingest Eviction Lab tracking data (external-data:eviction-lab)
description: Registry entry eviction-lab (attribution required). Partial court coverage by design — record coverage explicitly per geography so absence is never read as zero filings (BB-051 no-false-absence rule).

title: Ingest USDA Food Access Research Atlas (external-data:usda-food-access)
description: Registry entry usda-food-access (public domain, tract, 2019 atlas on 2010 tract geography — crosswalk needed against 2020-tract collections).

title: Ingest sundown-town dataset via exclusion-infrastructure lane (external-data:sundown-towns-osf)
description: Registry entry sundown-towns-osf (verdict UNVERIFIED — terms review first). MUST go through the BB-082 exclusion-infrastructure lane with historic-safety/source-registry.ts dignity rules (preserve possible/probable/surely confidence labels); deliberately excluded from launch-corpora by lane rule.

title: Ingest LEHD LODES employment flows (external-data:lehd-lodes)
description: Registry entry lehd-lodes (public domain, block-level, LODES8 on 2020 geography). Origin-destination employment flows; aggregate to tract/county for the map layers rather than persisting 100M+ block pairs raw.

title: Ingest CDC PLACES tract health estimates (external-data:cdc-places)
description: Registry entry cdc-places (public domain, tract/county/place). Modeled prevalence ~40 measures; tract GIS-friendly CSV has all measures per row. Joins acsTractProfiles by tract GEOID (2020 boundaries for 2024+ releases).

title: Ingest SEDA education archive (external-data:seda-education-archive) — rights-gated
description: Registry entry seda-education-archive: CC BY-NC 4.0 + data-use agreement (verdict noncommercial). RIGHTS REVIEW + agreement acceptance required BEFORE download; keep Firestore collection closed like holcAreas.

title: Ingest CRDC school civil-rights data (external-data:crdc-school-civil-rights)
description: Registry entry crdc-school-civil-rights (public domain, school-level). Discipline/arrests/course-access by race; joins national-catalog districts via NCES ids. Older collections have stable ZIPs (2013-14/2015-16/2017-18); newer on civilrightsdata.ed.gov/data.

title: Ingest HUD CHAS housing-burden tables (external-data:hud-chas)
description: Registry entry hud-chas in packages/domain/src/external-data-sources.ts (public domain, tract/county/state). Cost burden + overcrowding by income and race; ACS-derived so tract vintage matches the CHAS release. Same ingest pattern as ingest-opportunity-atlas.ts.

title: Ingest HMDA loan-level snapshots (external-data:hmda-loan-level)
description: Ingestion order 1 of the remaining external sources (owner list 2026-07-18). Registry entry: packages/domain/src/external-data-sources.ts id hmda-loan-level (public domain, tract+county, annual snapshots). Pattern: packages/firebase/scripts/ingest-opportunity-atlas.ts (raw artifact to Storage raw-sources/, curated Firestore subset with provenance quartet + datasetChecksum, contentHash idempotency, rules closed until surface exists). Mortgage denial/origination by race joins acsTractProfiles via tract GEOID — check boundary vintage per HMDA year.

title: Mobile app foundation: portable tokens, API contract, MapLibre Native spike
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §4 'Mobile app readiness').

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('mobile ready — will support web and mobile app later, iOS and Android').
Size: L (spike + contracts, not the apps themselves)
Dependencies: repo-b5a06269 (the PMTiles basemap is the shared map asset).

Why: the web experience must not paint the native apps into a corner. The cheap insurance is keeping three contracts portable NOW: design tokens, the public read API, and the map stack.

Deliver:
- Token pipeline: packages/ui tokens (--bp-*) exported to a platform-neutral JSON (style-dictionary-compatible shape) generated from the same source, committed, CI-checked against drift.
- API contract doc: the read-only public projections surface (active release, entities, search) written down as the mobile data contract — shapes already validated by publicEntityProjectionSchema; document auth posture (public read + App Check).
- MapLibre Native spike: render the archive-styled PMTiles basemap + entity circles on one iOS simulator screen; findings memo (what ports cleanly: style JSON yes, DOM markers no -> symbol layers).
- Screen map: §6 surface inventory annotated with native-first notes (sheets, deep links blap://entity/{id}).

Never: shipping app-store artifacts under this bead; forking the token source; building native-only design patterns the web doesn't share.

Acceptance:
1. tokens.json generated + drift-checked in CI. 2. Contract doc reviewed against a real projection read. 3. Spike memo with screenshot committed to docs/research. 4. No web regression (pure additive).

Model routing: fast model for pipeline/doc; spike on a strong model only if the Native style port hits judgment calls.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: Map street-level rebuild: full basemap, cities, mobile sheets, clustering polish
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §5 is the binding spec).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('the map has to be first class as users may be using this to visit places they find here' + 'rip whatever needs to be ripped').
Size: XL
Dependencies: none hard; census choropleth (repo-4274f931) and address search layer on top of this basemap.

Why: readers will physically visit the places they find — a marker on a Montgomery corner, Vernon AME in Greenwood, a Nashville campus. The current boundaries-only ink plate with a z12 ceiling cannot orient a person on foot. The archive look survives as a STYLE applied to real geography, not a ceiling on it.

Deliver:
- Self-hostable vector basemap (Protomaps PMTiles preferred; OpenFreeMap acceptable — free-first, no per-tile vendor fees, no API-key lock-in) served from our infra, restyled into the archive treatment: ink plate, muted street casings + names, city/neighborhood place labels, civic anchors; commercial POI noise suppressed. National frame to street level (maxZoom 18).
- City labeling at locality zooms from the same tiles (replaces nothing — state labels stay; the archive stays the subject, streets are context).
- Keep the persistent-canvas architecture (ADR-017), camera presets, kind-encoded circles, county/state hairline system, cluster expansion, density tiers — these ride ON the new basemap unchanged.
- Mobile: consolidate explore floating panels into bottom sheets; one-handed reach; 44px+ targets at street zooms; glare-legible in both themes (on-site use).
- Clustering polish at the new zoom range (spiderfy or expansion behavior past clusterMaxZoom where multiple records share a building).
- Performance: PMTiles range requests, tile cache headers, cold-load budget measured at 375px mid-tier device.

Never: per-tile commercial vendor dependency as the only path; POI/commercial clutter; losing the dignity rules at street zoom (public precision still governs — street-level BASEMAP does not mean street-level RECORD precision for redacted records); breaking reduced-motion.

Acceptance:
1. A visitor at z17 in Montgomery can read street names and orient to the Rosa Parks arrest-site marker record with the archive visual language intact. 2. All 104+ release records render on the new basemap with existing encodings; clusters decompose within two interactions everywhere. 3. Explore panels become bottom sheets at <=48rem, verified 375px both themes. 4. Basemap serves from our own hosting with no per-request vendor fees. 5. Lighthouse/trace budget recorded at national frame and street zoom. 6. BB-051 dignity re-verified at street zooms.

Model routing: strong model for basemap style authoring + sheet UX; fast model for tile-hosting plumbing and config.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: rebrand-gate-probe
description: Temporary probe that new issues use repo- prefix; close immediately.
close_reason: Prefix probe only; confirmed repo- ids.

value: Brand kit transparent exports (brand/logos, brand/symbols) came from a broken background-removal pass — dark variants had white/paper artwork keyed out entirely and grain speckle across the canvas; light lockup had opaque near-white residue in letter counters. Canonical rebuild path: re-matte from brand/source/approved-reference.png (uniform ~white bg) with difference-key alpha + unpremultiply, then derive dark variants by remapping the neutral axis ink->archivePaper (#F7F3EE), white->white, copper untouched. Always QA logo assets by compositing over ink AND paper at true display scale (~240px lockup, 44px symbol) — full-size reads over checkerboard hide keyed-out white regions.

Course-correction wave 2026-07-17: created BB-067..BB-085 (brand/pigment mark, design language v2, story, map platform, vector search via Firestore KNN gemini-embedding-001@768d, RRF hybrid retrieval, discovery adapters RSS/IA/DPLA/Wayback + gated Reddit + web-search/CommonCrawl, community submissions w/ consensus review, UGC compliance/living-person ethics, three-project env isolation, cloud apply, tracker hygiene, relevance feedback, historic-safety layered scoring (crime stats NEVER in composite), citation integrity/link-rot w/ Try-searching-for pattern, scheduled automation backbone, operator lane w/ .claude/skills as thin callers). BB-051 un-deferred to P0 XL national map experience. Labels: course-correction. Owner priorities: design first (BB-067/068), free-first everywhere, self-updating, U.S. only. Second adversarial pass in progress: fact registry (BB-086), legal corpus + free-source change monitoring (BB-087), editorial trust surfaces (BB-088), resilience red-team (BB-089) — protection framed as rigor/trust, never defensiveness.

@repo packages, ds- CSS tokens, APP_ env prefix are brand-agnostic and must not be renamed for product rebrands. Product display name (BlackStory) and brand assets live in @repo/config/identity + brand/. Beads never appear in apps/packages source, UI copy, or error strings. Tracker prefix is repo-. Prod GCP project id black-book-efaaf is immutable. New GCP projects use repo-staging/repo-internal.

Client bundles must never import @blap/domain barrel — use subpaths (@blap/domain/map/geography, taxonomy/topics, trust, disclaimers, facts, geography/*, entity-status, entity-kinds, map/map-source, map/decade-presence). Barrel pulls publication → node:crypto.

GCS on black-book-efaaf: black-book-efaaf-public-media (public media) and black-book-efaaf-raw-sources (created 2026-07-18: US-CENTRAL1, uniform access, publicAccessPrevention enforced) for raw dataset archives under raw-sources/<source>/<version>/ with .sha256 sidecars. There is NO default .firebasestorage.app bucket.

Location accuracy: Census street geocode + file cache; never auto-snap when matched street suffix disagrees (Motown Blvd→St); neighborhood drift threshold 1600m (~1 mile); named places that Census cannot resolve stay review (repo-ftt1).

Multi-agent bead execution pattern: (1) bd ready first; claim with bd update --claim before work. (2) Model selection: pick the first capable model from a cost/efficacy standpoint, not a blanket tier-by-category rule -- start from the cheapest/fastest model that can competently handle the SPECIFIC task, escalate only when that task's actual complexity/ambiguity/risk demonstrates need. 'Touches security code' or 'touches core-domain logic' alone does not justify a stronger tier if the task itself is mechanical/well-specified (implement against an already-locked design, follow an established pattern). Reserve escalation for genuine judgment-under-ambiguity: novel design decisions, threat-modeling from scratch, resolving conflicting requirements. Corrected 2026-07-17 after over-routing to a top tier by category alone. (3) Strict exclusive path ownership per agent; parent owns plan.md and packages/*/src/index.ts barrel merges. (4) Agents export from local barrels only; parent merges export * after completion. (5) Fix exactOptionalPropertyTypes with conditional spreads, not notes: undefined. (6) No live GCP/Firebase apply; document human cloud steps. (7) Close beads when repo acceptance met even if cloud console steps remain (same as BB-021/024). (8) Launch newly unblocked beads immediately when deps close. (9) Validate package tests+typecheck before claiming parent done. (10) Use bd not TodoWrite; update plan.md coordination tables.

Redesign tranche (BB-096..BB-101) hard requirement from owner 2026-07-17: the app must be FULLY RESPONSIVE across screen sizes and device types — every surface verified at minimum 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop); touch targets >=44px; no horizontal page scroll; wide content scrolls in its own container; map experience keeps full functional parity on mobile (tap targets, sheet UI); fluid type scale carries the range. Treat responsive breakage as a P0 finding in the BB-101 gate.

UCR hate crime data has NO county field — only agency ORI. Join to county via ucrAgencies crosswalk: FBI agency API (https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/agency/byStateAbbr/{ST}) gives county name + lat/lng per ORI; resolve by name when unambiguous, else point-in-county on agency coords against apps/web/public/geo/us-counties-20m.geojson. That lifts incident resolution from 83% to 98.2% (fixes NYPD/DC/Columbus/Portland/Dallas, which are multi-county or 'NOT SPECIFIED').

Wave 2026-07-17 parallel: BB-019 strong model owns publication/release; BB-021 and BB-037 on fast models for surface/adapter scaffolding; BB-012 deferred ADR-011; BB-048 web polish parallel on apps/web only. Parent merges domain/firebase barrels.

Census API key: 1Password item 'USCensus' (Private vault, field credential) = Google Secret Manager secret census-api-key on black-book-efaaf (v1, hash-verified 2026-07-18). Scripts read CENSUS_API_KEY from env (root .env.local locally), caller-supplied to @blap/domain census adapters; provenance sourceUrl is always the keyless form.

Public list/search: prefer gs://…/public/releases/{releaseId}/entities.json + search-index.json (or local fixtures), then unstable_cache Firestore; never rebuild search from entity scan when publicSearchIndex exists. Deploy rules to deny client projection reads.
Wave2: BB-020 backup, BB-022 hosting, BB-023 armor, BB-024 appcheck(stronger), BB-038 query-packs; exclusive paths; parent merges barrels.

BlackStory @repo/web on port 3048: NEVER kill/restart if curl http://127.0.0.1:3048/ returns HTTP 200. Agents repeatedly killed healthy next-dev (TERM/KILL) causing churn; Next itself was not crashing. Policy: health-check first; if 200 report PID+URL and exit; only kill when curl fails AND listener is hung; never spawn parallel kill+restart subagents; EADDRINUSE means reuse the running server.

Human-bar directive (owner 2026-07-18, binding on ALL redesign/bead work): surfaces must feel human — docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b is the canonical statement. Every surface bead is judged against: (1) FINDING — >=3 honest paths to every record (map, search, browse/story), dead ends are defects; (2) TRANSITIONS — context carries across navigation (camera, focus, scroll, back); (3) NUANCE — rewarded hover/press, voice-written empty/loading states, deliberate discovery moments; (4) SMOOTHNESS — prefetch, compositor-only animation, <100ms perceived; (5) EASE — payoff action one tap away, shareable URL state, forgiving inputs. Bar conflicts with a bead's Deliver list escalate to owner, never ship silently.

Owner directive 2026-07-17: 'Everything will be in Firebase for now.' Binding: application infra + data + all ingestion (legal APIs, discovery adapters, fact registry) stay Firebase/GCP-native — no multi-cloud data architecture, no required external dependency to ship. The red-team's Cloudflare-in-front (DNS/edge/origin-hiding) and warm off-Google backup are recorded in BB-089 as OPTIONAL hardening with explicit trigger conditions (a takedown/DDoS event, or a high-profile launch), NOT launch-blocking work. Firebase-native protections are primary: App Check + budget kill-switch + degraded snapshot mode (BB-033/035) are the true availability backstop; Firestore PITR/backup (BB-020). Registrar/DNS choice and root-account 2FA are not 'leaving Firebase' — they're account/domain hygiene and stay pre-launch. Reconciles with earlier 'id like to stay in firebase, you have full judgement.'
Wave3 add-on: BB-025 rate limits starts after BB-024; owns security rate limit package + API quota helpers; BB-026 remains blocked until BB-025 closes.

2020/dec/pl race variables: P1_003N is White alone, P1_004N is Black or African American alone — the census-demographics adapter shipped with P1_003N and the fail-closed assertVariableLabels caught it live 2026-07-18 before any bad data landed. Always verify decennial variable ids against the dataset's own variables.json.

Owner operating principle (2026-07-17): this project gets limited time and money — it must mostly run itself. Binding implications: (1) free tiers and free data sources first, paid only with explicit owner sign-off; (2) every recurring job is budget-capped with a kill switch and fails quiet-but-visible (review queue + digest, never pages); (3) prefer one-time setup over recurring labor; (4) automation proposes, owner disposes in periodic sessions — review queues must be batchable in minutes; (5) scope beads lean — reuse existing packages/models before building parallel systems; (6) when a bead offers a gold-plated vs good-enough path, take good-enough and record the upgrade trigger.

Tract-keyed collections must carry explicit tractVintage: ACS 2020s releases use 2020 tracts (acsTractProfiles), Opportunity Atlas uses 2010 tracts (opportunityAtlasTracts) — never join without a crosswalk. acsTractProfiles/opportunityAtlasTracts/holcAreas are client-CLOSED in firestore.rules; censusCountyDecades and acsCountyProfiles are public-read.
BB-026 started after BB-025: search/query guardrails on fast model; owns security query guardrails + api-public search guards; BB-040/041/029 ready remain candidates.
Wave3: BB-027 admin auth (stronger), BB-028 web security (fast), BB-039 discovery (fast). BB-024 still in flight; BB-025 waits on it.

FBI CDE bulk downloads use short-lived SIGNED URLs, not stable links. Resolve one with: GET https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/s3/signedurl?key=<awsFile>. The awsFile keys come from https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/assets/JSON/downloads/downloads.json (hate crime = additional-datasets/hate-crime/hate_crime.zip). The api.usa.gov/crime/fbi/cde upstream was fully down (503) on 2026-07-18; cde.ucr.cjis.gov serves its own API fine.

Owner file-naming preference: lowercase-kebab file names everywhere, including docs and asset packs (e.g. readme.md, asset-manifest.json, black-book-brand-guide-final.pdf) — no uppercase file names in new work. Applied to brand-system/ on 2026-07-17.
